title: Ali Bedni Bugyal Trek
description: A 6-day trek to India's grandest twin alpine meadows, Ali and Bedni Bugyal, offering close-up views of Mt Trishul and Mt Nanda Ghunti. The trek starts and ends at Lohajung, Uttarakhand, passing through ancient oak forests and lush high-altitude meadows at 12,550 ft.
highlights: Indiahikes Community Campus at Lohajung with Mt Nanda Ghunti views
Ancient Giant Oak forests near Wan's Latu Devta Temple
Ali and Bedni Bugyal – India's grandest twin alpine meadows
Close-up views of Mt Trishul (7,000m) and Mt Nanda Ghunti (6,000m)
Meadows lush green in summer, golden in autumn, and snow-draped in winter
Trek through Ghairoli Patal forests
price_includes: Accommodation (Swiss tents at Lohajung Campus and four-season tents on trek)
All meals from dinner Day 1 to dinner Day 5
Safety equipment (helmets, gaiters, micro-spikes as required)
Medical safety gear and oxygen cylinders
price_excludes: Transport to and from basecamp (₹3,000 for both ways, +5% GST)
Cloakroom beyond one piece of luggage (₹500 per additional piece)

Today, our cosy Community Campus welcomes you here with Mt Nanda Ghunti on the horizon.The Oak Forests towards Ghairoli Patal: Trek through an ancient forest of Giant Oaks. Near Wan’s Latu Devta Temple, you’ll see tree trunks, wide enough to need five people holding hands to wrap around them.Walk on India’s Grandest Meadows: Ali and Bedni bugyal are our favourite meadows. Lush green in summer, a golden hue in autumn, and snow-draped in winter, these meadows are postcard-perfect in any season.Close-Up Views of Big Mountains: At Ali and Bedni Bugyal, you’re up close and personal with Mt Trishul and Mt Nanda Ghunti. These are 7,000 and 6,000-metre summits rising before you from bottom to top. In such cases, Indiahikes will make every effort to offer you a safe alternative trek. If that isn't possible, we will cancel your trek and issue a voucher for the trek fee in line with our standard cancellation policy.However, if you would like to receive a full refund of your fee under such circumstances, opt for the Indiahikes Shield while registering. This shield grants you the option to receive a refund in case your trek is cancelled due to external reasons. However, you will need a small day pack.Find details about the Indiahikes Offloading Process here.Trek InclusionsThe Indiahikes Experience A Specially Designed Trek to Ali Bedni Bugyal: Take on the adventure of trekking to Ali Bedni Bugyal, India's grandest twin alpine meadows, supported by a carefully crafted trek plan that not only ensures safety but also enables:Connection to Yourself: In a world that rarely slows down, the trek’s remote setting offers a chance to disconnect. Indiahikes Trek Leaders, backed by their facilitation training, help you look inward and build a deeper connection with yourself.Connection to Nature: All Indiahikes Trek Leaders are trained naturalists, skilled in delivering insightful storytelling sessions on the natural elements you see on the trek.Connection to Others: The trek is designed to foster teamwork, turning strangers into a close-knit group through shared challenges and meaningful experiences.AccommodationLohajung Campus: Stay in Swiss tents at the exclusive Indiahikes Community Campus. The campus features the vibrant Urjino Café, Crosstrek Store, as well as dedicated breakout areas for relaxation

title: Bijli Mahadev Trek
description: A 2-day easy weekend trek near Manali for beginners, leading to the famous Bijli Mahadev Temple along a scenic and less-trodden route. Ideal for those seeking a short Himalayan experience with professional support and guided nature storytelling.
highlights: Best weekend trek near Manali for beginners
Trek to the famous Bijli Mahadev Temple
Scenic and less-trodden route
Trained naturalist Trek Leaders
Four-season tents with -10°C sleeping bags
Highest altitude of 8,120 ft
Suitable for ages 7 and above
10 km total distance
Facilitation training for self-connection and teamwork
price_includes: All meals from breakfast Day 1 to evening snacks Day 2
Access to Indiahikes Jungle Line Campus
price_excludes: Transport to/from Manali Beas Bridge (₹1,000 optional, ₹500 each way)
Cloakroom for extra luggage beyond one piece (₹500 per extra item)
Travel from hometown to pickup point

itinerary.title: Arrival at Indiahikes Jungle Line Campus & Trek Begins
activities: Arrival at Manali Jungle Line Campus
Camp setup and overnight stay in tents
itinerary.description: Reach the Indiahikes Community Campus (Jungle Line) by 7 AM. Have breakfast at the Urjino Café and begin the trek after orientation. Trek towards the campsite.

itinerary.title: Summit to Bijli Mahadev Temple & Return
activities: Drop-off to Manali Beas Bridge by 9 PM
itinerary.description: Trek to the famous Bijli Mahadev Temple and descend back to Karate village for drop-off transport to Manali Beas Bridge.

title: Brahmatal Trek
description: A 6-day trek to a high-altitude lake at 12,250 ft in the shadow of Mt Trishul, starting and ending at Lohajung basecamp. Suitable for trekkers aged 8 and above with an easy-moderate difficulty level.
highlights: Trek to a high-altitude lake at 12,250 ft
Views of Mt Trishul
Camping in four-season tents
Trained naturalist trek leaders
Local guide experience
Suitable for ages 8 and above
Indiahikes Community Campus stay at Lohajung
22 km total trek distance
price_includes: Accommodation in tents (2-sharing) and Swiss tents at Lohajung Campus
All meals from dinner Day 1 to dinner Day 5
Safety equipment (helmets, gaiters, micro-spikes when required)
price_excludes: Transport to and from basecamp (₹3,000 for both ways, +5% GST)
Extra cloakroom luggage beyond one piece (₹500 per item)

itinerary.title: Arrival at Lohajung
itinerary.description: Arrive at Lohajung basecamp. Stay at Indiahikes Community Campus. Dinner included.

itinerary.title: Trek begins from Lohajung
itinerary.description: Begin the trek from Lohajung basecamp towards the higher camps.

itinerary.title: Continued Trek
itinerary.description: Continue trekking through the Himalayan terrain towards Brahmatal.

itinerary.title: Continued Trek
itinerary.description: Further progress on the trail towards Brahmatal lake.

itinerary.title: Brahmatal Summit/Lake and Return
itinerary.description: Trek to Brahmatal high-altitude lake in Mt Trishul's shadow and begin return journey. Dinner at Lohajung.

itinerary.title: Departure from Lohajung
itinerary.description: End of trek at Lohajung. Dropoff at Live Free Hostel, Rishikesh by 7:00 PM.

title: Channarayana Durga Weekend Trek
description: A 2-day easy weekend trek to an ancient fort offering dominating views, featuring guided nature walks, magical sunrise and sunset experiences, and facilitated reflections in the outdoors.
highlights: Climb to an ancient fort with dominating views
Magical sunrise and sunset guided nature walks
Facilitated reflections connecting you with nature
Trek gear rentals available
Suitable for ages 7 and above
Fresh wholesome vegetarian meals cooked on-site
price_includes: Vegetarian meals (lunch, tea snacks, dinner, breakfast)
price_excludes: Transport to and from Indiahikes Southern Campus

Rent Here.SUITABLE FOR7 years and aboveTrek InclusionsThe Indiahikes experienceOur trek experience goes beyond a trek. With every step of the way, we connect you to nature and your inner self. Trek to Channarayana Durga: Experience the adventure of climbing to an ancient fort with a group with whom you form long-lasting bonds. Magical Sunrise & Sunset Walks: Immersive guided nature walks that reveal the hidden intricacies of the natural world around you.Facilitated Reflections: Conclude your adventure with meaningful reflections designed to help you reconnect with yourself and nature on a deeper level.Who goes with you on the trek?Trained Personnel:A professional Trek Leader, also serving as your lead facilitator, nature guide and storyteller, ensures the trek is enriching.Additional Facilitators (1 for every 10 trekkers) to ensure personalised attention and support.A dedicated kitchen support team to prepare fresh, wholesome meals.AccomodationStay in sturdy four-season tents (two trekkers per tent) designed for comfort in all weather conditions.Nutritious & Delicious Meals:Vegetarian meals are freshly cooked on-site, including lunch, tea snacks, dinner, and breakfast.Top-Quality GearAll essential camping gear is provided, along with a fully equipped medical kit and safety equipment.Trek ExclusionsTransportTransport to and from the Indiahikes Southern Campus. There is a parking slot available near the campus. You can come in your own vehicle too - https://goo.gl/maps/gLyr1h1VeiGADtQ47Anything apart from inclusionsTerms and Conditions1. Understanding the Risks of Trekkingexpand accordionI understand that trekking is an adventure sport that involves:Natural Hazards:Extreme Weather: Sudden rainfall, flash floods, hailstorms, thunderstorms and lightning strikes. It might even cause your trek to be called off without notice. Wildlife Encounters: Encounters with dangerous animals from the animal kingdom such as bears, snakes, arthropods, insects, ticks, etc can pose a serious threat. Encounter with different kinds of plants. Touching, eating, or any kind of interaction with a toxic, poisonous, or harmful plant can pose a serious threat too. Natural Disasters: Landslides, earthquakes, avalanches, and other natural disasters can occur in mountainous regions, posing significant risks.Trail-Related risks and personal injuries:Slips and Falls: Uneven terrain, slippery surfaces, and smooth or loose rocks can increase the risk of falls, accidental drownings, and other injuries.Accidents Involving Equipment: Improper use or malfunction of equipment such as climbing gear or trekking poles can lead to accidents.Accidents that may cause emotional trauma, disfigurement; temporary or permanent disability, including paralysis. Injuries Caused by Third Parties: Accidents or injuries caused by other trekkers or individuals in the area.Exposure to Harmful Elements: Prolonged exposure to the sun, cold, or other elements can cause sunburn, dehydration, heat cramps or other heat-related illnesses.Medical Emergencies:Limited Access to Medical Facilities: Remote trekking areas often have limited access to hospitals, clinics, or medical professionals.Difficulty in Evacuation: Evacuating an injured person from a remote location can be challenging and time-consuming.Other Risks:Civil Disturbances or War: Political instability or conflicts in the region can pose security risks.Terrorism: Terrorist activities can threaten the safety of trekkers.Social or Labor Unrest: Protests or strikes can disrupt travel plans and create safety concerns.Equipment Failures: Gear failure or malfunction can lead to accidents or discomfort.Transportation Issues: Delays, cancellations, or accidents involving transportation can affect the trek.Unexpected Events: Unforeseen circumstances such as weather changes, road closures, transportation failures or natural disasters can impact the trek.Diseases: Trekkers may be exposed to diseases or infections prevalent in the region.By participating in this trek, you acknowledge that:I am aware of these risks.I am voluntarily taking part in the activity.I will follow the safety guidelines provided.I am responsible for taking care of the equipment I use during the trek. I understand Indiahikes’ employees are qualified but have difficult jobs to perform. They seek safety

title: Dayara Bugyal Trek
description: An ideal Himalayan trek for beginners and families, covering 21 km over 6 days through undulating meadows, oak and rhododendron forests, with sweeping views of the Gangotri and Yamunotri ranges. The trek is based out of Raithal and reaches a highest altitude of 11,830 ft.
highlights: Indiahikes Community Campus at Raithal with views of Mt. Srikanth, Gangotri massif and Draupadi ka Danda
Undulating Dayara Bugyal meadows with sheep grazing in the distance
Sweeping views of Yamunotri and Gangotri ranges including Mt. Srikanth, Jaonli, Bandarpoonch
Romantic forests of oak, pine and rhododendrons
Suitable for beginners and families, including children aged 8 and above
Highest altitude of 11,830 ft over 21 km
price_includes: Accommodation in tents (2-sharing) including Swiss tents at Raithal Campus
All meals from dinner on Day 1 to dinner on Day 5
Safety gear (helmets, gaiters, micro-spikes when required)
price_excludes: Transport to and from basecamp (₹2,600 round trip / ₹1,300 one-way + 5% GST)

itinerary.title: Arrival at Raithal Basecamp
itinerary.description: Arrive at Raithal Campus. Dinner included. Stay in Swiss tents at the Indiahikes Community Campus.

itinerary.title: Trek Day
itinerary.description: Begin trekking through forests of oak, pine, and rhododendrons toward Dayara Bugyal meadows.

itinerary.title: Trek Day
activities: Views of Gangotri and Yamunotri ranges
itinerary.description: Continue trek across undulating Dayara Bugyal meadows with panoramic Himalayan views.

itinerary.title: Trek Day
itinerary.description: Explore higher reaches of Dayara Bugyal with views of Mt. Srikanth, Jaonli, Draupadi ka Danda, and Bandarpoonch.

itinerary.title: Return Trek
itinerary.description: Begin descent back toward Raithal through forests. Dinner at Raithal included.

itinerary.title: Departure from Raithal
itinerary.description: Trek concludes at Raithal. Drop-off at Asli Pappu Da Dhaba, Dehradun at 6:00 PM.

title: Dayara Bugyal Trek
description: An ideal 6-day Himalayan trek for beginners and families starting and ending at Raithal, covering 21 km and reaching a highest altitude of 11,830 ft. The trek features sweeping meadows, oak and rhododendron forests, and panoramic views of the Gangotri and Yamunotri ranges.
highlights: Indiahikes Community Campus at Raithal with views of Mt Srikanth, Gangotri massif and Draupadi ka Danda
Undulating meadows of Dayara Bugyal
Sweeping views of Yamunotri and Gangotri ranges including Bandarpoonch and Jaonli
Forests of oak, pine and rhododendrons
Suitable for beginners and families (8 years and above)
Highest altitude of 11,830 ft
21 km trek over 6 days
price_includes: Accommodation in tents (2-sharing) and Raithal Campus Swiss tents
All meals from dinner Day 1 to dinner Day 5
Safety gear (helmets, gaiters, micro-spikes when required)
Cloakroom facility (one piece of luggage per trekker)
price_excludes: Transport to/from basecamp (₹2,600 for both ways)
Extra cloakroom luggage beyond one piece (₹500 per item)

itinerary.title: Dayara Bugyal

title: Deoriatal Chandrashila Trek
description: A 6-day moderate trek culminating in a summit climb to Chandrashila (12,083 ft), offering one of India's best panoramic views including Chaukhamba, Nanda Devi, and nearly 20 Himalayan peaks. The trek covers 32 km starting and ending at Sari village, with highlights including Deoriatal Lake, Rohini Bugyal meadows, and rhododendron forests near Chopta.
highlights: Indiahikes Community Campus at Sari village with view of Chandrashila summit
Chaukhamba's reflection in Deoriatal Lake
Chandrashila summit panorama – Chaukhamba, Nanda Devi, and ~20 Himalayan peaks
Rohini Bugyal meadows opening in dense jungle on Day 3
Rhododendron-covered hillsides near Chopta and Tungnath in spring
Excellent birdwatching opportunities
Trained naturalist trek leaders
price_includes: Stay at Indiahikes Community Campus (Sari) in gender-specific dorm rooms
All meals from dinner on Day 1 to dinner on Day 5
Safety equipment (helmets, gaiters, micro-spikes when required)
price_excludes: Transport to and from basecamp (₹2,650 optional)

itinerary.title: Trek through Rohini Bugyal
itinerary.description: Continue the trek through dense jungles, with the surprise opening of the meadows of Rohini Bugyal.

itinerary.description: Trek through the stretch between Chopta and Tungnath, known for hillsides covered in pink and red rhododendrons, especially beautiful in spring.

itinerary.title: Summit Day – Chandrashila
itinerary.description: Summit climb to Chandrashila (12,083 ft) for a panoramic view of Chaukhamba, Nanda Devi, and nearly 20 Himalayan peaks across Garhwal and Kumaon. Dinner at Sari campus.

activities: Dropoff at Live Free Hostel, Rishikesh
itinerary.description: Trek concludes at Sari. Trekkers depart; dropoff at Live Free Hostel, Rishikesh by 6:00 PM.

title: Kanamo Peak Trek
description: A 7-day high-altitude summit climb to Kanamo Peak (19,553 ft) in the heart of Spiti Valley, starting from Kibber village. One of the highest trekkable summits in India, offering 360-degree views of 35 major peaks across Ladakh, Spiti, Kullu, and Kinnaur.
highlights: Summit Kanamo Peak at 19,553 ft — higher than any peak in Europe
No technical skills required for the summit
360-degree views of 35 major peaks across Ladakh, Spiti, Kullu, and Kinnaur
Iconic drive across Rohtang Pass and Kunzum La through Hikkim, Langza, and Komic
Start trek from Kibber — authentic Spiti village with mud-brick homes and monasteries
Snow leopard country in Kibber
Stay at Indiahikes Jungle Line Campus in Manali amid deodar forests
Trained naturalist Trek Leaders and local guides
Oxygen cylinders and comprehensive medical kit throughout trek
price_includes: Stay in Swiss tents at Indiahikes Community Campus in Manali
All meals from lunch on Day 1 to breakfast on Day 7
Safety equipment (helmets, gaiters, micro-spikes when required)
One piece of cloakroom luggage storage
price_excludes: Transport Manali to Kibber and back (₹4,800 + 5% GST optional)
Extra cloakroom luggage beyond one piece (₹500 per item)
Any expenses not mentioned in inclusions

itinerary.title: Trek begins from Kibber

itinerary.title: Trek to Kanamo Base Camp

Views of Parang La and Pin Parvati Pass on clear days
itinerary.description: Summit Kanamo Peak at 19,553 ft, standing higher than every peak in Europe. Enjoy 360-degree views of 35 major peaks across Ladakh, Spiti, Kullu, and Kinnaur.

itinerary.title: Trek back towards Kibber
itinerary.description: Descend from Kanamo Base Camp back towards Kibber.

itinerary.title: Departure from Kanamo Base Camp — Return to Manali
activities: Dropoff at Beas Bridge, Manali by 8 PM
itinerary.description: Breakfast at Kanamo Base Camp before returning to Manali. Pickup at Beas Bridge, Manali at 8 PM.

title: Kedarkantha Trek
description: The Kedarkantha Trek is considered the finest summit climb for beginners, offering snow-draped forests, scenic clearings, and a rewarding Himalayan summit experience in winter. The 6-day, 23 km trek is operated by Indiahikes from their community campus at Kotgaon, Uttarakhand.
highlights: Indiahikes Community Campus at Kotgaon overlooking terraced fields and traditional homes
Clearings in dense forests creating miniature meadows
Snow-draped forests of oaks, maple, and conifers in winter
Kedarkantha Summit Climb at 12,500 feet
First winter trek opened by Indiahikes in 2011
Suitable for beginners and trekkers 8 years and above
price_includes: Accommodation in Swiss tents at Kotgaon Campus and four-season tents on trek (2-sharing)
All meals from dinner on Day 1 to dinner on Day 5
Safety gear (helmets, gaiters, micro-spikes when required)
Cloakroom facility (one piece of luggage per trekker at no charge)
price_excludes: Transport to and from basecamp (₹2,600 for both ways, +5% GST)
Extra cloakroom luggage beyond one piece (₹500 per additional luggage)

itinerary.title: Trek through forests and clearings

itinerary.title: Summit climb

itinerary.description: Depart from Kotgaon back to Dehradun. Drop-off at Asli Pappu Da Dhaba, Dehradun at 6:00 PM.

title: Miyar Valley Trek
description: A 7-day moderate trek through the heart of Lahaul, featuring high-altitude glacial river deltas, wildflower meadows, and a rich mix of Buddhist and Hindu culture. The trek covers 60.2 km with a highest altitude of 13,202 ft, starting and ending at Khanjer.
highlights: High-altitude river delta landscape with sweeping green meadows beyond Khanjer
Crossing icy glacial streams and tributaries of the Miyar River
Mixed Buddhist and Hindu culture near Yolling with stupas and temples side by side
Wildflower beds in the cold desert landscape of Lahaul
Views of renowned climbing peaks: Fortress Group, Tharang Massif, and Menthosa
Highest altitude of 13,202 ft
Trek distance of 60.2 km

Trek in a high-altitude outwash plain, crisscrossed by countless glacial streams.Crossing Icy Glacial Streams: The trek constantly weaves across icy glacial streams and tributaries of the Miyar River. title: Nag Tibba Weekend Trek

Weekend trek suitable for beginners (11 years and above)

Nature storytelling sessions by trained naturalist trek leaders

Oxygen cylinders and medical safety gear throughout the trek

title: Pin Bhaba Pass Trek
description: A 9-day dramatic crossover trek covering 50 km from the lush Bhaba Valley to the stark cold-desert landscape of Lahaul, passing through Kashmir-like meadows, river deltas, and a high-altitude pass at 16,105 ft. Often described as the most dramatic crossover trek in India.
highlights: Forests on the way to Mulling with turquoise river views
Kashmir-like meadows at Karah
Camping on a rare river delta at Phutsirang
Dramatic crossover at Pin Bhaba Pass (16,105 ft)
Contrast between lush Bhaba Valley and stark cold-desert Lahaul
Quaint village of Mudh as the finishing point
New landscape experience every single day
price_includes: Accommodation (homestay at Yangpa + tents on trek, 2-sharing)
All meals from dinner on Day 1 to dinner on Day 7
Safety equipment (helmets, gaiters, micro-spikes when required)
price_excludes: Transport to/from trek base (₹4,500 optional add-on)
Travel from hometown to Shimla pick-up point
Day 1 Shimla to Yangpa transport (₹1,800 + 5% GST per person)

itinerary.title: Arrival at Yangpa Basecamp
itinerary.description: Arrive at Yangpa in the Bhaba Valley. Stay in an authentic homestay. Dinner included.

itinerary.title: Trek to Mulling
activities: Forest walk through oaks and pines
itinerary.description: Trek under canopies of huge oaks and pines with a turquoise river flowing deep down the valley. Climb up a narrow ledge to the huge open grasslands of Mulling.

itinerary.title: Trek to Karah
itinerary.description: Reach the Kashmir-like meadows of Karah, wide and grassy and ringed by peaks — arguably the finest campsite setting on this trek.

itinerary.title: Trek to Phutsirang
itinerary.description: Camp on a rare Himalayan river delta at Phutsirang with braided channels, flat ground, and mountains closing in on all sides.

itinerary.title: Approach to Pin Bhaba Pass
itinerary.description: Continue ascending towards the Pin Bhaba Pass, gaining altitude through high terrain.

itinerary.title: Pin Bhaba Pass Crossing (16,105 ft)
itinerary.description: Cross the dramatic Pin Bhaba Pass at 16,105 ft, transitioning from the lush green Bhaba Valley to the stark, pink cold-desert landscape of Lahaul.

itinerary.title: Trek to Mudh Village
itinerary.description: Descend to the quaint village of Mudh in Pin Valley. Dinner at Mudh is the last included meal.

itinerary.title: Buffer / Travel Day
itinerary.description: Buffer day for contingencies or travel towards Manali.

itinerary.title: Drop-off at Beas Bridge, Manali
activities: Drop-off at Beas Bridge by 8 PM
itinerary.description: Trek concludes with drop-off at Beas Bridge, Manali by 8 PM.

itinerary.title: Arrival at Yangpa
itinerary.description: Pick-up from Old Bus Stand, Shimla at 6:30 AM and drive to Yangpa basecamp. Stay in an authentic homestay experiencing the culture of the Bhaba Valley region. Dinner included.

itinerary.title: Trek to Mulling
activities: Forest trek through oak and pine canopy
itinerary.description: Trek under canopies of huge oaks and pines with a turquoise river flowing deep down the valley. Climb up a narrow ledge to the huge open grasslands of Mulling.

itinerary.title: Trek to Karah
itinerary.description: Reach the wide, grassy Kashmir-like meadows of Karah, ringed by peaks. Argued to be the finest campsite setting on the trek.

itinerary.title: Trek to Phutsirang
itinerary.description: Camp on a rare Himalayan river delta at Phutsirang, with braided channels, flat ground, and mountains closing in on all sides.

itinerary.title: Pass Crossing Day
itinerary.description: Cross Pin Bhaba Pass at 16,105 ft — a dramatic crossover from the lush green Bhaba Valley to the stark, pink cold-desert landscape of Lahaul.

itinerary.title: Descent towards Mudh
itinerary.description: Continue descent through the Pin Valley cold desert landscape towards the village of Mudh.

itinerary.title: Arrival at Mudh
itinerary.description: Reach the quaint village of Mudh. Dinner at Mudh is included as the final covered meal of the trek.

itinerary.title: Drive to Manali

itinerary.title: Drop-off at Manali

title: Valley of Flowers Trek
description: A 6-day moderate trek to the UNESCO World Heritage Site Valley of Flowers, featuring over 500 documented flower species and a visit to Hemkund Sahib Gurudwara at 14,107 ft. Operated by Indiahikes with base camp at Karchi, Uttarakhand.
highlights: Indiahikes Campus at Karchi beneath Dronagiri Peak
Trek through UNESCO World Heritage Site Valley of Flowers with 500+ flower species
Seasonal blooms from July to September with new flowers each week
Sit by the Pushpavati Nala Riverbed at the far end of the valley
Trek to Hemkund Sahib Gurudwara at 14,107 ft beside a glacial lake
Rare Brahmakamal blooms on the Hemkund Sahib trail in late monsoon
price_includes: Accommodation (Swiss tents at Karchi Campus and lodges on trail)
All meals from dinner Day 1 to dinner Day 5
Sleeping bags (-10°C rated), hygiene liners, sleeping tents, foam mats
Safety equipment (helmets, gaiters, micro-spikes when required)
price_excludes: Transport to/from basecamp (₹3,400 for both ways, +5% GST)
Cloakroom beyond one piece of luggage (₹50 per extra piece)
raw_text: <iframe src="https://www.googletagmanager.com/ns.html?id=GTM-WKDG2F6" height="0" width="0" style="display:none;visibility:hidden"></iframe>Just launched: A New 4-Day Unexplored India Travel Programme. This time, it's Unexplored KonkanView UnexplorationRequest A CallbackPhotosscroll to photosValley of FlowersA Legendary Trail the World Wants to TrekWe have announced dates for 2026 to Valley of Flowers-Hemkund Sahib. Please scroll down to find the dates. Register soon as this is one of the fastest treks to fill. Click here to get personal help in planning this trek.TREK DIFFICULTYModerateTREK DURATION6 days / 38 kmHIGHEST ALTITUDE14,107 ftSUITABLE FOR11 years and aboveBASECAMPKarchi, UttarakhandACCOMMODATION TYPEGender-specific homestay/lodgeFITNESS CRITERIA5 km in 40 minsPICKUP DETAILSLive free hostel, Rishikesh at 5.30 AMDROPOFF DETAILSLive free hostel, Rishikesh at 6.00 PMPACKING CHECKLISTClick to GenerateCLOAKROOMAvailableOFFLOADINGAvailableHighlightsThe Indiahikes Campus at Karchi: At our Karchi Campus, you are right beneath the gaze of Dronagiri Peak. Being this close to a Himalayan giant before your trek even begins is rare.Trek in India's Most Famous Valley: Discovered by accident, explorer Frank Smythe named it the Valley of Flowers. Today this UNESCO World Heritage Site with more than 500 documented flower species is among Himalaya’s most iconic treks.The Changing Colours of the Valley: From July to September, new flowers bloom each week. Even in June, before peak bloom, the lush greenery and mountain views are stunning.Sitting by the Pushpavati Riverbed: Trek to the Pushpavati Nala Riverbed at the far end of the valley. Not everyone makes it there — but just sitting by the river there, in complete silence is worth the extra effort.Trek to Hemkund Sahib: Perched at 14,107 ft beside a huge glacial lake, trek to one of the world’s highest Gurudwaras. Witness rare Brahmakamal blooms on this trail in the late monsoon.Trek Fee₹12,450Karchi to Karchi+5% GST+₹180 Trek Insurance view insurance info+₹3,400 for transport to and from basecampOptional Additions₹1,390Indiahikes Shieldview optional additions₹800Backpack Offloadingview optional additionspro trekker philosophy eligibleOne step closer to our Pro-Trekker path: your 5th trek is free with us. These situations are not within Indiahikes' control. Before you register, please be prepared for the possibility that your trek might be called off due to these uncontrollable factors. In such cases, Indiahikes will make every effort to offer you a safe alternative trek. If that isn't possible, we will cancel your trek and issue a voucher for the trek fee in line with our standard cancellation policy.However, if you would like to receive a full refund of your fee under such circumstances, opt for the Indiahikes Shield while registering. This shield grants you the option to receive a refund in case your trek is cancelled due to external reasons. Note: Shield Refunds will not be applicable once the trek has begun, even if it cannot be completed due to unforeseen circumstances, including but not limited to adverse weather, wildlife movement, political unrest, or other such reasons.Read before you offloadWhen you offload, a mule or porter will carry your belongings.Offloading is for those with injuries, health issues or other limitationsBook via Your Profile after trek registration. However, you will need a small day pack.Find details about the Indiahikes Offloading Process here.Trek InclusionsThe Indiahikes Experience A Specially Designed Trek to Valley of Flowers: Take on the adventure of trekking to Valley of Flowers, A legendary trail the world wants to trek, supported by a carefully crafted trek plan that not only ensures safety but also enables:Connection to Yourself: In a world that rarely slows down, the trek’s remote setting offers a chance to disconnect. title: Delhi-Leh-Umling La-Srinagar Bike Backpacking Trip
destination: Delhi to Leh to Umling La to Srinagar
description: A thrilling 13-night bike backpacking expedition from Delhi through Manali, Leh, Umling La (world's highest motorable road), Nubra Valley, Pangong Lake, Kargil, and ending in Srinagar. Operated by Just Wravel with professional tour leaders, backup vehicles, and medical support for high-altitude safety.

9–10 years of operator experience on this route

raw_text: Call Us+91 97 11 74 18 22Search tripsLogin“Went for bike tour from delhi-srinagar via manali,leh, hanle, kargil and so on, very professional team from mechanic to ride marshal everyone did their part very well, especially vishal, arvind,adnan and all, they made sure everything goes as planned, timing and everything was perfectly planned, nothing was missed from the itinerary which was given, made some new friends, good memories and bit of backpain😜…. You can happily go with them without any second thought, here are some pics too🦦”koushik Vadla“An Unforgettable Experience with Just Wravel – DLUS (Delhi-Leh-Umlingla-Srinagar) I recently completed the 13-night DLUS (Delhi-Leh-Umlingla-Srinagar) trip with Just Wravel, and I must say—it was an incredible and seamless experience from start to finish. Right from the get-go, everything that was promised was delivered. My friend and I had opted for the Dual Rider package, and the bike provided was in excellent condition. The presence of a backup vehicle for repairs or emergencies added an extra layer of comfort and safety throughout the journey. One thing I really appreciated was the flexibility Just Wravel offered. If anyone felt unwell or tired, they were allowed to switch from the bike to the tempo traveller. The team was clearly well-prepared for such situations, with backup support staff ready when needed. Our Tour Leader, Vishal Tiwari, and Bike Marshal, Arvind, were both outstanding—extremely friendly, supportive, and professional. They gave detailed briefings every night, walking us through the next day’s route, key spots to cover, and even advice on layering up appropriately for the weather. This kind of planning really made us feel well looked after. What stood out the most was their seriousness about our health and safety, especially given the high altitudes of Leh and Umlingla. From the beginning of the trip in Manali, we were mentally prepared for potential mountain sickness, and the team carried ample medical supplies, including oxygen cylinders. I personally experienced altitude sickness, and I’m especially grateful to Vishal Tiwari, who provided me with timely and effective medical support. The whole journey felt like we were in the hands of a seasoned and experienced team—and that’s exactly what they are. Having run this route for 9–10 years, their experience shone through in every aspect of the trip. If you're looking for a well-organized, safe, and thrilling adventure through the mountains, I highly recommend Just Wravel. They truly go the extra mile to ensure your trip is memorable—for all the right reasons.”Samit Abhishek“The Delhi-Leh-Srinagar trip was amazing! His guidance was a big reason the trip went so smoothly. He was always there to help, especially if someone felt sick or tired due to the high altitude. He kept us motivated throughout the journey and never let anyone feel left out or low. Overall, it was a fantastic experience, and I’m happy to have made so many new friends during this trip. title: All Girls Leh Ladakh Trip with Hanle Umling La
description: An exclusively curated all-girls road trip through Leh–Ladakh covering iconic high-altitude passes, Nubra Valley, Pangong Lake, Changthang Valley, Hanle, and the world's highest motorable road at Umling La. The journey blends adventure, cultural exploration, and sisterhood across 8 days of raw Himalayan beauty.

title: Bhutan Bike and Backpacking | 8 Days Bhutan Bike Tour
description: An 8-day fully-supported Bhutan bike tour starting from Bagdogra, covering high-altitude passes like Chele La (13,000 ft) and Dochula Pass, iconic cultural sites including Tiger's Nest Monastery and Punakha Dzong, and scenic valleys like Phobjikha. The trip blends adventure riding with Buddhist heritage and natural Himalayan beauty.

Ride to Phobjikha Valley via Dochula Pass (10,200 ft) and visit Gangtey Monastery
Explore Punakha Dzong and walk across the Punakha Suspension Bridge
Ride through Chele La Pass at 13,000 ft

Fully-supported ride with backup vehicles, experienced bike marshals, and medical/mechanical support
10,000+ reviews on platform

title: Leh Ladakh Bike Trip from Srinagar with Hanle and Umling La
description: A thrilling motorcycle expedition covering the Srinagar–Leh–Umling La–Delhi circuit, passing through iconic high-altitude passes like Zoji La, Khardung La, and the world's highest motorable road at Umling La. The trip blends adventure riding with cultural experiences, remote stays, and guided support from experienced trip leaders.

Small group format for a close-knit experience

highlights: Cross Umling La — world's highest motorable pass at 19,024 feet

title: Leh to Leh Bike Trip with Hanle Umling La Tso Moriri - 9 Days Backpacking Tour
description: A 9-day action-packed backpacking and bike tour from Leh covering some of Ladakh's most remote and breathtaking destinations including Nubra Valley, Pangong Lake, Hanle, Umling La (world's highest motorable road), and Tso Moriri Lake. The trip blends rugged high-altitude adventure with cultural immersion and scenic Ladakhi landscapes.
highlights: Khardung La Pass – one of the highest motorable roads in the world
Umling La – world's highest motorable road
Pangong Lake – iconic crystal-clear blue waters
Nubra Valley – Bactrian camel ride on Hunder Sand Dunes
Hanle – India's largest astronomical observatory and clear skies for stargazing
Tso Moriri Lake – remote high-altitude lake
Diskit Monastery with 100-foot Buddha statue
Changthang Valley and Plateau exploration
Leh local sightseeing including Magnetic Hill, Shanti Stupa, and Hall of Fame
Camping under the stars in Nubra Valley
10,000+ reviews

itinerary.description: Explore Hunder Sand Dunes with Bactrian camel rides, optional ATV rides, and enjoy desert landscape with snow-capped peaks. Night camping under the stars.

As the rains arrive, the entire state transforms into a world of roaring waterfalls, mist-covered hills, lush green valleys, and cloud-draped roads that feel straight out of a dream.From June to September, the abode of clouds changes completely, every landscape comes alive making Meghalaya one of the most sought-after monsoon destinations in India. Kudos to Just Wravel and the fantastic team for creating such a delightful travel experience! 🌍✨”Suman Rathod12+ Photos Meghalaya Kaziranga Backpacking Trip - Forests, Falls & Safari (6N/7D) (10000+ Reviews) ShareDetailsPickup & DropGuwahati → GuwahatiCategoryBackpacking TripsDuration7 DaysInclusionsMealsStaysTransfersActivitiesSafe TravelFlexible CancellationEasy EMICertified Captains24/7 SupportOverviewItineraryInclusions & ExclusionsCancellation PolicyThings To PackOverviewMeghalaya, tucked away in India’s northeast, is a state that feels like a world apart. Known as the “abode of clouds,” it’s famous for its dramatic waterfalls, living root bridges, clean villages, and lush green hills. The best way to reach Meghalaya is by flying into Guwahati, Assam, which is well-connected to major cities. From there, it’s a scenic drive into the heart of Meghalaya. The weather can be unpredictable—sunshine one moment, mist the next—so pack light but smart. Mobile connectivity is decent in most areas, but this is a great place to unplug and explore. Whether you’re traveling solo or with friends, Meghalaya’s mix of nature, culture, and adventure makes it a solid pick for a backpacking trip.Adding Kaziranga National Park to this itinerary makes the experience even more rewarding. Located in Assam, Kaziranga is a UNESCO World Heritage Site and home to the world’s largest population of one-horned rhinoceroses. It’s also known for its rich biodiversity, including tigers, elephants, swamp deer, and hundreds of bird species. An early morning Jeep Safari here offers a chance to witness wildlife in its natural habitat—something that adds a whole new dimension to your Northeast India adventure. Combining Meghalaya’s waterfalls and caves with Kaziranga’s wild terrain gives you the best of both worlds: nature’s serenity and raw wilderness.Now, if you’re wondering how to make this trip smooth and memorable, that’s where JustWravel steps in. Our Backpacking Trip to Meghalaya & Kaziranga is designed for travelers who want the best of the region without the stress of planning. With top-notch stays, comfortable transportation, and a trained Trip Captain and Trip Lead guiding you throughout, you’ll be in good hands. The itinerary includes adventure activities, immersive local experiences, and plenty of opportunities for community bond

You guys made our travel smooth and memorable.”Jui Prabhu6+ Photos Thailand - Phuket Krabi Group Tour with Full Moon Party (6N/7D) (10000+ Reviews) ShareDetailsPickup & DropPhuket → PhuketCategoryBackpacking TripsDuration7 DaysInclusionsMealsStaysTransfersActivitiesSafe TravelFlexible CancellationEasy EMICertified Captains24/7 SupportOverviewItineraryInclusions & ExclusionsCancellation PolicyThings To PackOverviewExploring Thailand with the right group, who share the same kind of energy, is the best way to get the actual feel of the island. And if chilling on beaches, dancing together on a full moon night, and creating endless memories with the right group is on your list, then join this Thailand group tour.This 6 Nights 7 Days Thailand Group tour takes you through Phuket, Krabi, Koh Samui, Koh Phangan, and Phi Phi Islands, covering five of Southern Thailand’s most popular islands and coastal destinations. With island-hopping tours, the Full Moon Party at Haad Rin Beach, and an Ark Beach Club fire show all on the itinerary, this is one of the most complete ways to see southern Thailand in a single group departure.5 Destinations, One Group Tour - Phuket, Krabi, Koh Samui, Koh Phangan & Phi Phi IslandsThis group tour covers five of southern Thailand's most visited coastal destinations — each with a noticeably different character. Phuket serves as the entry point and the base for the Phi Phi Islands excursion. Krabi is where the 7-island tour takes place — limestone karsts, clear waters, and an open coastline. Koh Samui brings beach time by day and Ark Beach Club's fire show by night. Koh Phangan is the Full Moon Party at Haad Rin Beach. And Phi Phi Islands — Maya Bay, Pi Leh Lagoon, and Phi Phi Don — close the trip before departure.Unlike a single-destination holiday, this Thailand group tour covers five destinations in one planned journey. Transfers, ferries, and activities between destinations are all arranged in advance, which removes the biggest logistical challenge of multi-island travel in Thailand and lets the group focus entirely on the trip.Highlights of This Thailand Phuket Krabi Group Tour7-Island Krabi Tour with Beachside BBQThe Krabi 7-island tour covers Phra Nang Cave Beach, Tup Island, Mor Island, Chicken Island, Poda Beach, and Tan Ming Island in one full day on the water. The tour includes snorkeling at multiple spots, low-tide sandbar walks, and a BBQ dinner as the sun sets over the Andaman Sea. It is one of the most popular island-hopping day trips in Krabi and one of the better ways to spend a day as a group.Full Moon Party at Haad Rin Beach, Koh PhanganThe Full Moon Party in Koh Phangan is the highlight of this trip. Held every month on Haad Rin Beach, it is one of the most well-known beach parties in the world — fire performers, music running across the beach, and an atmosphere that carries through the night. Group ferry and land transfers to Koh Phangan are arranged as part of the tour. Entry tickets are payable separately at the venue.Ark Beach Club Fire Show, Koh SamuiKoh Samui adds a different side to Thailand’s nightlife scene. title: Zanskar-Hanle-Tso Moriri Bike & Backpacking Trip
description: A 10-day adventure through Ladakh's remote landscapes covering Zanskar Valley, Tso Moriri, and Hanle via high-altitude passes, ancient monasteries, and glaciers. Travelers can choose between biking on a Royal Enfield Himalayan or backpacking, with all meals, stays, and permits included.
highlights: Ride to Umling La or Mig La, among the world's highest motorable roads
Explore remote Zanskar Valley and Phuktal Monastery built into a cliff
Camp under starry skies at Sarchu
Visit Tso Moriri and Tso Kar high-altitude lakes
Cross multiple high passes including Pensi La, Shinku La, Nakee La, and Lachung La
Stargazing at Hanle, home to India's largest astronomical observatory
Spot rare black-necked cranes
View the massive Drang Drung Glacier
Visit ancient monasteries including Lamayuru and Thiksey
Royal Enfield Himalayan BS6 bike provided with full riding gear
price_includes: Royal Enfield Himalayan BS6 bike with fuel (for bike option)
riding gear (helmet, knee guard, elbow guard, gloves, jacket)
MAP meal plan (18 meals - breakfasts and dinners)
price_excludes: early check-in before 11 AM or late check-out after 8 AM
additional accommodation or food due to travel delays
spare parts cost for accidental damage to motorbike
motorbike transfer or towing if dropped on route
lunches and meals not mentioned in inclusions

costs due to flight cancellations, landslides, roadblocks, or natural calamities

itinerary.title: Arrival in Leh | Acclimatization Day
itinerary.description: Arrive in Leh, rest and acclimatize to the high altitude. Meet the trip leader for a briefing, enjoy a warm dinner, and prepare for the journey ahead.

itinerary.title: Leh to Kargil (215 km)
activities: visit Hall of Fame war memorial
itinerary.description: Ride from Leh to Kargil with stops at key landmarks including Sangam river confluence, Hall of Fame war memorial, Magnetic Hill, Gurudwara Pathar Sahib, Lamayuru moonland, and Fotula and Namika La passes.

itinerary.title: Kargil to Padum, Zanskar Valley (230 km)
itinerary.description: Drive into Zanskar Valley reaching Padum, its main town. Pass through Rangdum village, cross Pensi La Pass, view Statso and Langtso Lakes, and see the Drang Drung Glacier before settling into a homestay.

itinerary.title: Padum to Purne (56 km)
itinerary.description: Travel to Purne village via the cliff-carved Phuktal Monastery, one of the most remote monasteries in Ladakh with ancient caves. Relax at a Purne homestay.

itinerary.title: Purne to Sarchu (159 km)
itinerary.description: Depart from Purne passing through Kargyak village (last village of Zanskar), see Gombo Rangzan holy mountain, cross Shinku La Pass, and join the main Manali-Leh highway near Darcha before reaching Sarchu camps.

itinerary.title: Sarchu to Tso Moriri (220 km)
itinerary.description: Drive past Puga Valley hot springs, navigate the Gata Loops, cross the More Plains, and traverse Nakee La and Lachung La passes. Stop at Tso Kar Lake before arriving at the serene Tso Moriri Lake.

itinerary.title: Tso Moriri to Hanle (180 km)
itinerary.description: Ride to Hanle, a high-altitude village on the ancient Silk Route. Spot rare black-necked cranes and visit India's largest astronomical observatory, ideal for stargazing.

itinerary.title: Mig La / Umling La Excursion
activities: ride to Umling La or Mig La
itinerary.description: Full-day excursion to Mig La or Umling La, among the world's highest motorable roads. Ride through open terrain with panoramic views and take photos at the summit.

itinerary.title: Hanle to Leh (260 km)
itinerary.description: Return to Leh passing through Chumathang hot springs, visiting Shey Palace and Thiksey Monastery, and browsing Leh's local markets before a final dinner.

itinerary.title: Departure from Leh
itinerary.description: After breakfast, transfer to the airport for departure, concluding the 10-day Ladakh adventure.

raw_text: Call Us+91 97 11 74 18 22Search tripsLogin“I went to Zanskar trip and it was crazy .we enjoyed alot both team member Ashish and shubham both are like brother We really enjoyed every moment.”Girish Gadage“This review is regarding Zanskar expedition. Zanskar Valley(Ladakh) is a remote location. The trip was very well managed and taken care of. Captain Shubham and Ashish really made sure that everyone is at good health as ladakh is at higher altitude. They provided all the information in the briefing. I had a great trip. Feedback - Kargil stay(caravan sarai) wasn’t good. It would be great if they change it as it wasn’t hygienic.”Pooja Wagh“Leh-leh (Hanle) by Kavindra This trip was a completely different experience. It was adventurous as well as beautiful with breathtaking experiences. JustWravel team was supportive in the entire journey.👍”Mansi agarwal“I travelled with Justwravel to Leh with Hanle for 8N/9D and I had the best experience. The services were amazing- be it hotels, rooms, traveller, bike ride- everything was just perfect. Ramneek and Manjunath were trip leaders and they were the best. They took good care and got the best medical care from both of them. They bought a cake and celebrated my birthday with full excitement. Really looking forward for my next trip with Justwravel and hopefully Ramneek and Manju.”Swati Gupta“Just did the Leh- umling La Hanle trip with Justwravel. It was truly an amazing journey. Team Leader Shashank and Ramneek were great and extremely knowledgeable with a lot of experience. The entire trip was full of unexpected adventure and a lot of fun. Will definitely plan out Tawang with the same team.”Ved Sharma10+ Photos Zanskar-Hanle-Tso Moriri Bike & Backpacking Trip (10000+ Reviews) ShareDetailsPickup & DropLeh → LehCategoryBackpacking TripsDuration10 DaysInclusionsMealsStaysTransfersActivitiesSafe TravelFlexible CancellationEasy EMICertified Captains24/7 SupportOverviewItineraryInclusions & ExclusionsCancellation PolicyThings To PackOverviewGet ready for an unforgettable 10-day journey through Ladakh's wild beauty, where you ride bikes or backpack across high mountains, sparkling lakes, and ancient villages. This trip takes you from Leh deep into Zanskar Valley, up to Hanle and Tso Moriri, and back again with a perfect mix of thrill, nature, and culture for bike fans, group travelers, or solo explorers. Over 9 nights, you'll cross high passes, see massive glaciers, visit quiet monasteries, and camp under starry skies, creating memories that last a lifetime.Your adventure starts in Leh, the high desert town full of fresh air and stunning views. Day 1 is all about resting and getting used to the altitude after you arrive. Relax at your hotel, drink plenty of water, and meet your trip leader for a fun briefing about the days ahead. Enjoy a warm dinner and a good night's sleep to build your energy.On Day 2, hit the road to Kargil after breakfast. Stop at Sangam, where two big rivers meet, and visit the Hall of Fame war memorial. Feel the magic at Magnetic Hill, where cars seem to roll uphill on their own, and pray at Gurudwara Pathar Sahib. Pass through Lamayuru's moon-like rocks and cross Fotula and Namika La passes for breathtaking sights. Check into your Kargil hotel for dinner and rest.Day 3 dives into Zanskar Valley, driving to Padum, its main town with old history and rock art. Go through Rangdum village, cross Pensi La Pass, and gaze at Statso and Langtso Lakes. See the huge Drang Drung Glacier up close before settling into a cozy homestay in Padum.Day 4 heads to Purne village. Along the way, explore Phuktal Monastery, built right into a cliff with caves used by wise men thousands of years ago. The rocky paths feel like stepping back in time. Relax in your Purne homestay with dinner.Day 5 takes you from Purne to Sarchu. Pass Kargyak, the last Zanskar village, see the holy Gombo Rangzan mountain, and climb Shinku La Pass, one of the highest roads around. Join the main highway near Darcha and reach Sarchu camps for a starry night.Day 6 brings you to Tso Moriri Lake. Drive past Puga Valley's hot springs, tackle the twisty Gata Loops, cross the wide More Plains, and go over Nakee La and Lachung La passes. Stop at Tso Kar Lake, then arrive at Tso Moriri's blue waters for a peaceful evening in cottages.On Day 7, ride to Hanle, a high village on the old Silk Route. Spot rare birds like black-necked cranes amid green fields and colorful hills. Hanle has India's biggest sky-watching observatory, perfect for stargazing. Stay in cottages or homestays.Day 8 is pure excitement: a trip to Mig La or Umling La, some of the world's highest roads you can drive on. Ride through open lands with amazing views, snap photos at the top, and return to Hanle buzzing with energy.Day 9 loops back to Leh. Pass Chumathang's warm springs, visit Shey Palace and Thiksey Monastery for a taste of Ladakh's kings and monks, then shop in Leh's lively markets. End with dinner at your hotel.Day 10 is departure

To save you the hassle, we have curated a selection of Leh Ladakh tour packages. Scroll below and choose a package that best suits your travel style. A visit to Ladakh is a feast for the eyes. Jagged mountain peaks, crystalline lakes and endless stretches of lush green meadows make up the ethereal topography of Ladakh. Known as the land of high passes, Ladakh lies in the northern part of Jammu and Kashmir. Once an important stopover for trade routes, Ladakh has slowly transformed into a spiritual hub. Monasteries are perched on the top of craggy barren hills while the mystical sound of prayer wheels reverberate across the quiet valleys of Ladakh. You can discover these picturesque spots in Ladakh with our Ladakh tour packages. The royal remnants of Ladakh are visible in its colossal forts and palaces dotting the arid landscape. You can find mammoth statues of Buddha along with countless fluttering prayer flags all around Ladakh, displaying a strong religious identity that Ladakh has formed throughout the years. This region is also known for its friendly locals. The generous Ladakhi community offers traditional homestays to tourists along with freshly-cooked Ladakhi cuisine. You can enjoy an insightful visit to Ladakh with our Ladakh tour packages and delve deeper into the history and culture of Ladakhi people. Ladakh Biking ToursEmbark on a biking expedition across the high passes of Ladakh. You can explore landscape and the zigzag roads of Ladakh on a bike. Ride on the highways of Ladakh that stretch through the barren Himalayan ranges. You can choose our Ladakh tour packages and enjoy a bike ride on some of the world’s highest motorable roads. Local Cuisine in LadakhOur Ladakh tour packages allow you to savour the authentic taste of Ladakh. You can try various local dishes such as thukpa, phirni, momos and yak cheese. Mildly flavoured, most of Ladakh's dishes are made up of vegetables such as pumpkins, potatoes, beans and beetroots. Many locals are generous enough to offer homemade dumplings to travellers. Ladakh is also famous for its delicious non-vegetarian Pulao. Ladakh also has its own local drink called Chhaang which is a barley-based beer that keeps you warm in the brisk winters of Ladakh. You can also explore restaurants such as Tibetan Kitchen, Blue Lotus and Summer Harvest with our Ladakh tour packages. Cafe culture is also thriving in the streets of Ladakh. You can visit Bob’s Cafe and OpenHand Cafe with our Leh Ladakh packages.Ladakh HighlightsSurrounded by the magnificent Karakoram Ranges, Ladakh’s landscape is an amalgam of natural vistas and awe-inspiring architectural marvels. Literally translating to the 'land of the kings', Rajasthan is one of the most visited destinations in India. Scores of tourists travel to this enchanting state to marvel at its gorgeous forts, restored palaces and bustling cities. Rajasthan tour packages are a wonderful way to explore the state since you can customise Rajasthan packages according to your preferences. Whether you'd like to cover Jaipur, Bikaner and Ajmer or explore the cities of Jodhpur and Jaisalmer – Rajasthan tour packages give you the freedom to choose. Tourism has always been an integral part of Rajasthan; so much so that the rush of tourists feels natural and almost familiar. Visitors will also be taken in by charming locals who usually double up as helpful guides when you are making your way through the maze of forts and palaces. An important part of medieval Indian history, Rajasthan is a land which promises abundant adventure, culture, craft and art. In fact, its bustling markets are one of the reasons that make Rajasthan so popular with tourists from across the world. From bandhani dupattas, colourful lamps, leather juttis to handmade home decor, meenakari jewellery, camel leather bags and lac bangles – there is tons to take back home. Travellers often claim that if one wants to truly disconnect, there is no better place than this cold desert. Many have described Spiti as vast fields of barren land shimmering in hues of brown and crimson, while others have compared its surreal beauty to a painting. Lying in the north-eastern part of Himachal Pradesh, Spiti and Lahaul are typically counted as one region, separated only by the massive Kunzum Pass, and the best way to explore the area is by booking personalised Spiti Valley tour packages. If it’s your first trip to the mountains and you are not familiar with the terrain, geography and way of life, Spiti Valley tour packages will make the trip far easier. Spiti, which means ‘middle land’, is true to its name as it is situated comfortably between India and Tibet and travellers will notice that the local culture has strong influences of both countries. Rudyard Kipling once said that Spiti is “a world within a world” and we completely agree. To explore the beauty of Spiti, one needs at least seven to fifteen days and we recommend booking Spiti Valley tour packages that give you this time. There are two ways to enter the district and travellers prefer exploring the valley in a circuit. With entry points from both Shimla and Manali, it is suggested that travellers can enter from one city and exit from the other to make the complete circuit. Fondly known as Little Tibet, this northern district has harsh climatic conditions and is accessible only during the summer season, making it essential that travellers plan their trip well in advance. To save costs on your travel and accommodation, it’s best to book Spiti Valley tour packages during the months of February and March to avoid last minute price surges. Surrounded by archaic monasteries, idyllic villages and sparkling blue rivers, Spiti is a dream to explore. A huge attraction for adventure enthusiasts from across the world, Spiti is sparsely populated and you can walk for miles without coming across another soul. This enchanting land is the base for some of the country’s best trekking trails and a trekking experience here is splendid. Situated at an altitude of 4,300 metres on the Samudra Tapu, this surreal lake is loved by trekkers and campers. Overlooking the Chandra River, it is one of the two high altitude wetlands in India (Tso Moriri being the other) which are considered to be Ramsar sites. The ever-changing hues of the lake is another reason why travellers look forward to camping trips here. Spiti Valley tour packages usually include the trek from Batal to Chandratal in Spiti holiday packages but you may need to customise the package according to your interests. Another place that you must include in your Spiti Valley tour is the Pin Valley National Park. Situated in the Cold Desert Biosphere Reserve, the park offers a fascinating experience. With a conflicted past and a thriving present, Turkey’s resilient spirit can be seen and felt in its cities and people. Its place as the sixth most visited country in the world is well-deserved as travellers can’t seem to get enough of its charming Mediterranean coastline, Greco-Roman villages and fairytales landscapes. The diversity of the country also extends to its people and culture. The Turks are one of the most welcoming people in the world and go out of their way to ensure visitors feel at home. The best way to discover this grand country is through Turkey tour packages. Not only are packages customised to your interests and budget, but you can be sure of covering the best places to visit in Turkey. If a rainbow-coloured, balloon sky is how you imagine Turkey to be, then Cappadocia should definitely be a part of your Turkey tour. Followed by the stunning beaches of Ölüdeniz and Alanya. For history buffs and architecture enthusiasts, the whole country is your muse. We recommend visiting the Roman ruins at Ephesus and also exploring the tenets of Sufism at Konya. Turkey tour packages offer adventure lovers a plethora of unforgettable experiences. From kayaking, diving, surfing to exploring the wetlands of Dalyan, river rafting in Çoruh River and trekking through the limestone peaks of ancient Lycia – adventure enthusiasts can expect a whirlwind of experiences. Istanbul, the unofficial capital, is one of the most popular tourist places in Turkey and any trip to the country is incomplete without a visit here. Turkey tour packages also take travellers through the popular cities of Antalya, Ankara and Bodrum. Extensive Turkey tour packages include all the popular places and things to do, however, it helps to remember that you can’t cover Turkey in one trip; it’s best to come back and explore the places you have missed. Starting and ending in Manali, this 8 to 11 days journey takes you through some of the most iconic and high-altitude landscapes in India. You begin by riding up from Manali into the mountains, crossing dramatic passes like Baralacha La and Tanglang La, and heading into the heart of Leh - the capital of Ladakh. From here, you continue the adventure over Umling La, known as one of the highest motorable passes in the world. The views from up here are wide, raw, and unforgettable. On this circuit, you also visit famous lakes like Pangong Tso, where the water changes colors with the light, and travel through Nubra Valley, known for its river valleys, desert patches and peaceful villages. Riding through high mountain roads, cold deserts, wide plains and big skies makes this route feel like a true Himalayan loop. This value-focused trip gives you long open roads, stunning landscapes, simple mountain stays and real adventure, perfect for travellers who want a full Ladakh experience without unnecessary extras. title: 11 Days Value Edition Ladakh Tour: Srinagar to Manali with Umling La
description: An 11-day Himalayan journey from Srinagar to Manali covering Ladakh's highest roads and untouched lakes, including Khardung La, Pangong Lake, Umling La (one of the world's highest motorable roads), and Tso Moriri. The trip traverses dramatic high-altitude landscapes, cold deserts, ancient monasteries, and remote villages.
highlights: Drive to Umling La — one of the highest motorable roads in the world
Pangong Lake — highest saltwater lake in the world
Tso Moriri — calm high-altitude lake with minimal crowds
Khardung La pass at 5359m
Nubra Valley cold desert and Hunder Sand Dunes
Bactrian camel ride in Nubra
Rezang La War Memorial
Monasteries in Leh and Diskit
Stargazing in Hanle
Zoji La and scenic Sonamarg meadows
Confluence of Indus and Zanskar rivers at Sangam Point
Srinagar to Manali full route through Sarchu

itinerary.title: Arrival in Srinagar – Day at Leisure
itinerary.description: Arrive in Srinagar, the summer capital of Jammu and Kashmir. Check in to the hotel, explore Srinagar Market on your own, and attend a briefing session with the Team Captain.

itinerary.title: Srinagar to Kargil via Sonamarg, Drass & Zojila
itinerary.description: Depart for Kargil covering approximately 200km in 8-9 hours. Cross the beautiful meadows of Sonamarg, conquer Zoji La pass (3528m), and pass through Drass village — the Gateway to Ladakh and one of the coldest inhabited places in India.

itinerary.title: Kargil to Leh – En Route Landmarks
itinerary.description: Drive from Kargil to Leh covering approximately 230km in 7-8 hours, crossing Namika La (3700m) and Fotu La (4108m) passes. Stop at Lamayuru, Sangam Point (confluence of Indus and Zanskar rivers), Magnetic Hill, and Pathar Sahib Gurudwara.

itinerary.title: Leh to Nubra Valley via Khardung La
itinerary.description: Drive from Leh to Nubra Valley covering approximately 125km in 5-6 hours via Khardung La (5359m), one of the world's highest motorable passes. Visit Shanti Stupa, Diskit Monastery, and Hunder Sand Dunes.

itinerary.title: Nubra to Pangong via Shyok Valley
itinerary.description: Drive from Nubra Valley to Pangong Lake covering approximately 160km in 5-6 hours through Agam and Shyok villages. Arrive at Pangong Lake (4300m) — the highest saltwater lake in the world, 136km long and 4km wide.

itinerary.title: Pangong to Hanle via Rezang La War Memorial
activities: Cross Loma Bridge on Indus River
itinerary.description: Drive from Pangong to Hanle village covering approximately 165km in 8-9 hours. Pass through Chushul and pay respects at Rezang La War Memorial (tribute to 1962 Indo-China War martyrs), then cross Loma Bridge on the Indus River.

itinerary.title: Day Excursion to Umling La | Visit Demchok
activities: Drive to Umling La (world's highest motorable road)
itinerary.description: Full-day excursion covering approximately 200km in 7-8 hours to Umling La, one of the highest motorable roads in the world, and visit to Demchok village.

On this 11-day journey you will travel from Srinagar to Leh, deep into Hanle, all the way to Umling La - one of the highest motorable roads in the world, and to the stunning blue waters of Tso Moriri, before ending in Manali. The trip begins in the green valleys of Srinagar and slowly climbs into the dramatic landscapes of Ladakh. You cross high mountain passes, drive through wide cold deserts, and witness changing colours of Pangong Lake. In Leh, you explore monasteries and local markets. In Hanle, you experience peaceful villages and clear night skies filled with stars. The real highlight is the drive to Umling La, where the air feels thin and the views feel endless. Standing there is a moment you will never forget. Tso Moriri adds another magical touch, with its calm waters surrounded by mountains and very few crowds. Long scenic drives, simple mountain stays, and raw landscapes make this journey special. If you are looking for a complete Ladakh trip package that covers Srinagar, Leh, Umling La, Tso Moriri, and Manali in one route, this adventure gives you the full Himalayan experience in its purest form. title: 3 Days Tirthan Valley Tour | Gushaini & Jalori Pass
description: A 3-day escape from Delhi to the serene Tirthan Valley covering a forest trek to Choie Waterfall, a trek to Serolsar Lake via Jalori Pass, and river rafting near Aut. The trip balances nature, adventure, and mountain calm without being overcrowded.
highlights: Overnight journey from Delhi into the Himalayas
Forest trek to Choie Waterfall in Gushaini
Trek to Jalori Pass and 360° Himalayan viewpoint
Visit to serene Serolsar Lake
River rafting near Aut
Bonfire and music evening in the mountains
Uncrowded, calm mountain experience
price_includes: Overnight travel Delhi to Aut and back (Semi-sleeper Volvo)
2 nights accommodation at resort/hotel in Gushaini
5 meals (Day 1: Breakfast & Dinner, Day 2: Breakfast & Dinner, Day 3: Breakfast)
Driver night charges, toll tax, parking charges

itinerary.title: Departure from Delhi
activities: Board vehicle at Delhi boarding point
itinerary.description: Reach your boarding point in Delhi by evening, board the vehicle, and begin the overnight journey towards Tirthan Valley.

itinerary.title: Arrival In Aut | Transfer To Gushaini – Choie Waterfall Trek
activities: Trek to Choie Waterfall through forest paths
Dinner and overnight stay in Gushaini
itinerary.description: Arrive at Aut by morning and transfer to Gushaini. After check-in and breakfast, head out for a scenic forest trek to Choie Waterfall, returning by evening for dinner and overnight stay.

itinerary.title: Jalori Pass – 360° Viewpoint – Serolsar Lake Trek
activities: Dinner and overnight stay in Gushaini
itinerary.description: After breakfast, drive to Jalori Pass and trek to the 360° viewpoint with panoramic Himalayan views, continuing to Serolsar Lake. Return to Gushaini for a relaxed evening with bonfire and music.

itinerary.title: River Rafting | Departure to Delhi
itinerary.description: After breakfast and hotel check-out, enjoy river rafting in the valley. Transfer to Aut by evening and board the overnight bus back to Delhi.

This 3 Days Tirthan Valley trip does exactly that, without trying too hard. It starts with an overnight journey from Delhi, and by morning, you’re deep in the mountains, reaching Aut and then moving into Gushaini. The shift is immediate. Cleaner air, quieter surroundings, and a pace that feels natural. The first day keeps it light with a forest hike to Choie Waterfall. It’s not just about the waterfall; the trail itself does most of the work, pulling you into the rhythm of the valley. Then things go a bit higher. A drive to Jalori Pass followed by a trek to Serolsar Lake gives you those wide, open Himalayan views. It’s calm, not crowded, and that’s the whole point. Before heading back, there’s just enough thrill with river rafting near Aut to break the stillness. What this trip really gets right is balance. It doesn’t try to show you everything; it just gives you enough space to actually feel where you are. title: 3D Bir Billing Escape: Paragliding, Rajgundha Valley & Prashar Lake
destination: Bir Billing, Rajgundha Valley & Prashar Lake
description: A 2-night, 3-day group trip from Delhi covering paragliding at Billing (world's second-highest takeoff point), the hidden semi-tribal Rajgundha Valley, and the mystical Prashar Lake with its 14th-century pagoda temple and floating island.
highlights: Paragliding at Billing — second-highest takeoff point in the world
Bird's-eye view of Bir valley and Dhauladhar range
Explore hidden semi-tribal Rajgundha Valley, untouched by commercial tourism
Hanumangarh trek from Chinna Pass with panoramic Himalayan views
Visit mystical Prashar Lake (2,730 m) with its floating island
14th-century pagoda-style Prashar Rishi Temple
Himalayan sunset from Bir Landing Site
Bonfire nights under star-studded skies
Tibetan cafés and local market in Bir
price_includes: Entire travel as per itinerary (Delhi to Bir and Mandi to Delhi in Semi-Sleeper Volvo)
2 nights accommodation (1N Swiss Camps at Bir, 1N homestay in Rajgundha)
Driver charges, toll taxes, parking, and vehicle-related expenses
price_excludes: Tips to helper, cook, guide or any staff
Costs due to natural calamity or forced circumstances

itinerary.title: Delhi To Bir | Overnight Journey
itinerary.description: Arrive at the designated pick-up point in Delhi by evening, meet the Trip Captain, complete boarding formalities, and begin the overnight journey toward the mountains.

itinerary.title: Arrival In Bir | Paragliding & Café Hopping
activities: Himalayan sunset from Landing Site / Sunset Point
itinerary.description: Reach Bir (1,525 m) by morning, check into campsite, have breakfast, then head to Billing (2,400 m) for a 15–30 minute paragliding flight. Afternoon spent exploring Bir Local Market and café hopping. Evening sunset from the Landing Site followed by bonfire and dinner.

itinerary.title: Bir To Rajgundha Valley | Hanumangarh Trek | Into The Hidden Highlands
activities: Scenic drive to Rajgundha Valley via Billing
Hanumangarh trek from Chinna Pass (3 km one side)
itinerary.description: After breakfast and checkout, drive 1.5–2 hours to Rajgundha Valley via Billing through oak and pine forests. Trek to Hanumangarh from Chinna Pass (3 km one side). Arrive in the semi-tribal village of Rajgundha by late afternoon and check into local homestay/campsite.

itinerary.title: Rajgundha To Prashar Lake | The Mystical Floating Island & Departure
itinerary.description: After breakfast, drive 4–5 hours to Prashar Lake (2,730 m). Explore the 14th-century Prashar Rishi Temple and the mysterious floating island. Drive ~2 hours to Mandi for dinner, then board overnight Volvo back to Delhi.

itinerary.title: Reach Back To Delhi By Morning
itinerary.description: Arrive back in Delhi by morning with memories from the mountains.

You’re at Billing on day one, the second-highest paragliding takeoff point in the world, strapped in and running off a cliff before your brain has fully caught up. That 15-30 minute flight over the valley and the Dhauladhar range below you, is a surreal experience you’ll never forget. The evening back in Bir feels earned. Bonfires, Tibetan cafés, monastery views, and a sunset from the landing site that the whole valley seems to stop for. Rajgundha Valley is the part most people have never heard of. It’s a semi-tribal village sitting quietly above the treeline, completely untouched by the kind of tourism that puts places on Instagram. Terraced fields, local homestays, mountain silence, and a sky so clear at night it feels almost unfair. Then you get to witness Prashar Lake which closes it out on its own terms. It’s a 14th-century pagoda-style temple sitting at 2,730 metres beside a glacial lake with a mysterious floating island that nobody has fully explained yet. It's the kind of place that makes the long drive feel like the point, not the inconvenience. With this Bir Billing group tour package, you get to explore three completely different worlds, beyond usual tourist trails. Seats are filling fast; book your spot today! title: 3D Kasol Kheerganga Trek: Ultimate Parvati Valley Group Trip

The trip includes an overnight stay in alpine camps at Kheerganga with bonfire, stargazing, and a visit to Manikaran Sahib on the return.

Explore Kasol's hippie cafes and Israeli food scene
Parvati River views

Board Volvo/Traveler for overnight journey to Delhi

In the Parvati Valley, Kasol is your base camp for everything chill. It’s a land of riverside cafes and hippie markets where you can spend hours just soaking in the "slow life." Just a bridge away lies the trail to Chalal, the favorite trail of travelers because it feels like a secret doorway into the wild. You’ll pass through ancient, towering deodar trees and hidden cafes and catch the best views of the roaring Parvati River. But the true heart of this journey is the 12 km trek to Kheerganga, featuring natural hot water springs, stunning Himalayan views, and a tranquil, spiritual atmosphere. The trail takes you deep into the raw mountain wilderness, past the thundering waterfalls and through forests that look like they belong in a movie. The climb tests your grit, but the reward is a massive meadow in the sky, surrounded by snow-capped giants. Once the sun dips, the mountain air gets sharp, and that’s when the bonfire becomes the center of everything. It’s just you, a hot meal, and a sky so clear you can actually see the Milky Way. You’ll be sleeping in alpine tents and waking up to the kind of 360-degree mountain views that you can’t put into words. Grab your spot today and let’s get this adventure on the road! Read MoreItineraryDay 0Arrival In Delhi & Overnight Journey To KasolArrive at the designated point in Delhi to meet our representative and your fellow travelers.Board the AC Volvo/Traveler and begin the scenic overnight journey toward the mountains.Spend the night bonding with the group and catching some sleep before reaching the valley.Day 1Arrival In Kasol & Visit To Chalal VillageWe’ll reach Kasol by morning, check into the hotel and freshen up.Have a hearty breakfast and spend some time exploring the Kasol market or the local hippie cafes for some Israeli food.Later, we’ll take a gentle hike (2-3 km) through the pine trees to Chalal Village, a quieter hamlet known for its laid-back atmosphere and valley views.Capture some of the best views of the valley on your way.Later, walk back to your hotel for an overnight stay in Kasol.Day 2Trek to Kheerganga. Overnight camping at Kheerganga.Wake up early morning and after having breakfast, head for Kheerganga.Trek will start from a village called Barsheni (22 km from Kasol) and you will be transferred to Barsheni in private vehicles.The Trek Trail of Kheerganga is 14 kms with a moderate difficulty level.Enjoy your walk in the heart of the beautiful Parvati valley with a stunning view of snowcapped mountains all around.Reach Kheerganga by evening.Have your dinner and enjoy the bonfire under (if weather permits) the sky studded with stars and snowcapped mountains all around.Sleep overnight in the camps and have an experience of a lifetime.Day 3Trek down to Barsheni. Departure to Delhi.Wake up early to see a beautiful sunrise while you sip on some hot mountain tea. After breakfast, we’ll start our descent back to Barshaini through the same scenic trail.Since it’s all downhill, you can take your time to click those landscape shots you missed on the way up.On the way back, we’ll visit Manikaran Sahib, known for its natural hot springs and spiritual atmosphere.Dip your feet in the warm water to relax your tired muscles before continuing toward Kasol.Reach Kasol by evening, freshen up, and get ready for departure.Board the Volvo/Traveler and begin the overnight journey back to Delhi.Reach Delhi by next morning as your mountain journey comes to an end.Day 4Rea

title: 3D Zanskar Trip from Manali | Gonbo Rongjon & Shinkhu La
description: A 3-day offbeat Himalayan expedition from Manali crossing the high-altitude Shinkhu La pass to reach Gonbo Rongjon, the sacred 'God's Mountain' rarely visited by travellers. Designed for those seeking a short yet powerful mountain experience without long leaves.
highlights: Reach Gonbo Rongjon – the sacred 'God's Mountain' rarely visited by travellers
Cross Shinkhu La (Shingo La) Pass at 5,091m – natural border between Himachal Pradesh and Ladakh
Drive through Atal Tunnel – world's highest road tunnel above 10,000 ft
Scenic Lahaul Valley landscapes and Chandra River views
Hike to Jogini Falls and explore Old Manali
Confluence of Chandra and Bhaga rivers at Tandi forming the Chenab River
Bonfire at Jispa Campsite
Short 3-day itinerary requiring minimal leave from work
price_includes: Entire travel as per itinerary (Delhi–Manali & Manali–Delhi in Semi-Sleeper Volvo)
4 meals (Day 1: Breakfast, Day 2: Breakfast + Dinner, Day 3: Breakfast)
Accommodation for 2 nights (1N hotel in Manali, 1N camps in Jispa)
Dedicated team captain throughout the trip
Driver charges, toll taxes, parking, and other vehicle-related expenses
price_excludes: Any food or beverages not mentioned in the package
Permits or entry tickets to viewpoints
Any other services or items not explicitly mentioned in inclusions

itinerary.title: Travel from Delhi to Manali
activities: Arrive at Delhi airport or railway station
itinerary.description: Overnight journey from Delhi to Manali by bus.

itinerary.title: Manali Local Sightseeing – Jogini Falls – Evening at Leisure

itinerary.description: Arrive in Manali, check in, and explore local attractions including a hike to Jogini Falls and Old Manali.

itinerary.title: Transfer to Jispa – Visit Gonbo Rongjon via Shinkhu La

Cross Shinkhu La (Shingo La) Pass at 5,091m
itinerary.description: Early morning drive through Atal Tunnel and Lahaul Valley, crossing Shinkhu La pass at 5,091m to reach the sacred Gonbo Rongjon, then return to Jispa/Keylong for overnight stay.

itinerary.title: Transfer from Jispa to Manali via Sissu
activities: Pitstop at Tandi – confluence of Chandra and Bhaga rivers forming Chenab River
Arrive in Manali in the evening
itinerary.description: Morning drive from Jispa back to Manali with scenic stops at Sissu, Atal Tunnel, and Tandi confluence, before boarding overnight bus to Delhi.

itinerary.title: Reach Delhi by Morning
itinerary.description: Arrive back in Delhi by morning, concluding the trip.

Think again.This unique Gonbo Rongjon expedition from Manali is designed for travellers who want to experience the raw beauty of Zanskar without taking long leaves. In just a few days, this journey takes you beyond the usual Manali circuit into landscapes that feel untouched and truly remote.The highlight of the trip is reaching Gonbo Rongjon, often called the “God’s Mountain” - a sacred and rarely visited peak that stands tall amidst dramatic Himalayan terrain. The journey itself is equally thrilling as you cross the mighty Shinkhu La, a high-altitude pass connecting Himachal to Zanskar.From driving through the iconic Atal Tunnel to witnessing the changing landscapes of Lahaul Valley, from serene stops at Sissu to the confluence at Tandi, this trip is packed with scenic moments and offbeat experiences.Perfect for those who seek something different, this short Himalayan escape offers adventure, exclusivity, and breathtaking views, all in a limited time. Jogini Falls.
