Visit Gonbo Rongjon via Shinkhu La.Wake up early in the morning and have your breakfast at the hotel in Manali.Get ready as we head out for a scenic long drive through the legendary Atal Tunnel, the world's highest road tunnel above 10,000 ft.We will then watch the landscape transform as we enter the Lahaul Valley.Drive along the mighty Chandra River and enjoy the views of the towering peaks at the backdrop. Continue riding through Jispa/Darcha region with minimal stops to maintain travel pace on this distance-heavy route.We will then pass the famous Shingo La Pass (also known as Shinku La) located at an altitude of 5,091m, that acts as a natural border between Himachal Pradesh and Ladakh.Arrive at Gonbo Rongjon and spend sometime exploring around, and clicking some pictures of the mighty “God’s Mountain”.Later, we will board our vehicle to drive back towards Jispa/Keylong by evening/night, concluding a full-day high-altitude excursion.Enjoy your dinner at the stay, after a long-day of sightseeing.Overnight stay in Jispa/ Keylong.Day 3Transfer from Jispa to Manali via Sissu.Wake up to a pleasant morning in Jispa and have your breakfast at the stay.Later, get ready and board your transfers to Manal.On this scenic drive to Manali, we will pass the picturesque village of Sissu and soak in the scenic landscapes. title: 5 Days Jibhi Manali Kasol Trip from Delhi
description: A 4-night, 5-day community group trip from Delhi covering Jibhi, Jalori Pass, Serolsar Lake, Manali, Atal Tunnel, Sissu, and Kasol. The trip blends scenic mountain views, light trekking, river rafting, and café hopping across Himachal Pradesh.
highlights: Visit to Jibhi Waterfall and Mini Thailand viewpoint
Trek to Serolsar Lake via Jalori Pass
River rafting on the Beas River in Manali
Drive through the iconic Atal Tunnel to Sissu
Café hopping in Old Manali and Kasol
Visit to Manikaran Gurudwara and hot springs
Optional hike to Chalal village
Bonfire and riverside relaxation in Gushaini

itinerary.title: Depart from Delhi
itinerary.description: Start from Delhi in the evening and travel overnight towards Tirthan Valley.

itinerary.title: Arrival in Tirthan – Waterfall Visit & Café Exploration
itinerary.description: Arrive at Aut, transfer to Gushaini, check in, visit Jibhi Waterfall, Mini Thailand viewpoint, and explore local cafés. Evening bonfire or riverside relaxation. Overnight stay in Gushaini.

itinerary.title: Jalori Pass – 360° Viewpoint – Serolsar Lake Trek
itinerary.description: Drive to Jalori Pass for panoramic views, then trek approximately 5 km to Serolsar Lake through dense forests. Return to Gushaini by evening. Overnight stay in Gushaini.

itinerary.title: Jibhi to Manali – Rafting & Café Hopping
itinerary.description: Check out from Jibhi and drive to Manali. Enjoy river rafting on the Beas River, then explore Old Manali's cafés and Mall Road. Overnight stay in Manali.

itinerary.title: Manali – Atal Tunnel – Sissu – Transfer to Kasol – Café Hopping
itinerary.description: Drive through the Atal Tunnel into Lahaul Valley, explore Sissu's waterfalls and mountain views, then travel to Kasol. Evening café hopping along the riverside. Overnight stay in Kasol.

itinerary.title: Explore Manikaran & Chalal | Kasol Market | Departure
activities: Board overnight Volvo bus to Delhi
itinerary.description: Visit Manikaran Gurudwara and hot springs, optional hike to Chalal village, explore Kasol markets, then board overnight Volvo bus back to Delhi.

itinerary.title: Reach back to Delhi
itinerary.description: Arrive back in Delhi by morning with trip memories.

This Jibhi, Manali, and Kasol trip is a great escape from Delhi or Chandigarh. Located in the peaceful region of Himachal Pradesh, Jibhi is known for its quiet surroundings, forest trails, and cosy cafés. The trip begins with an overnight journey, where the group comes together, and the travel experience starts. During the trip, you will explore the beauty of Jibhi with a visit to the serene Jibhi Waterfall and the unique Mini Thailand viewpoint. You will also head to Jalori Pass and go on a short trek to Serolsar Lake, walking through forest paths and enjoying wide mountain views along the way. The journey then continues to Manali, where you will experience river rafting in the Beas River and spend time exploring Old Manali’s cafés and local markets. The trip also includes a drive through the famous Atal Tunnel to Sissu, where you can enjoy stunning mountain views and waterfalls. Towards the end, you will reach Kasol, known for its laid-back vibe and riverside cafés. You will visit Manikaran and can take a short walk to Chalal village before heading back. From nature and adventure to café hopping and group bonding, this trip offers a perfect mix of experiences in the mountains. title: 6 Days Leh Trip with Nubra and Pangong | Super Saver
description: A budget-friendly 6-day Ladakh trip covering Leh local sightseeing, Nubra Valley via Khardung-La, and the iconic Pangong Lake via Chang-La. Ideal for friends, backpackers, and first-time travelers seeking an affordable Himalayan adventure.
highlights: Khardung-La crossing at 5359m – one of the highest motorable roads in the world
Nubra Valley cold desert and Hunder sand dunes
Double-humped Bactrian camel safari
Pangong Lake – highest saltwater lake in the world
Chang-La pass at 5360m
Shanti Stupa panoramic views over Leh
Sangam Point – confluence of Indus and Zanskar rivers
Hall of Fame museum
Gurudwara Pathar Sahib
Magnetic Hill optical illusion
3 Idiots movie set at Pangong Lake
Budget-friendly itinerary covering all major Ladakh highlights
price_excludes: Camel safari or ATV ride at Hunder

itinerary.title: Arrival in Leh – Leisure Day for Acclimatization
itinerary.description: Arrive at Leh airport after a scenic flight over the Himalayas. Meet the trip representative, transfer to hotel, check in, and rest. Take a short walk around the hotel for acclimatization. Trip captain provides a journey briefing in the evening.

itinerary.title: Leh Local Sightseeing
itinerary.description: After breakfast, explore Leh's iconic local attractions including Shanti Stupa, Gurudwara Pathar Sahib, Magnetic Hill, Sangam Point, and the Hall of Fame. Spend the evening exploring the Leh market, cafes, and local food.

itinerary.title: Leh to Nubra Valley via Khardung-La
itinerary.description: Drive from Leh over Khardung-La (5359m), one of the highest motorable roads in the world. Visit Diskit Monastery and explore Hunder sand dunes in Nubra Valley. Optional camel safari or ATV ride available.

itinerary.title: Nubra to Pangong Lake via Shyok – Explore the Colour-Changing Lake
activities: Drive through Agam and Shyok villages
itinerary.description: Journey from Nubra Valley to Pangong Tso via Agam and Shyok villages. Arrive at Pangong Lake (4300m), the highest saltwater lake in the world. Spend time at the lake, take photos at the 3 Idiots set, and check into a campsite nearby.

itinerary.title: Pangong to Leh via Chang-La (5360m)
activities: Drive through Karu, Thiksey, and Shey
Shopping and cafe hopping in Leh
itinerary.description: Wake up early to watch the sunrise over Pangong Lake. Drive back to Leh via Chang-La (5360m), passing through Karu, Thiksey, and Shey. Spend the evening exploring Leh market.

itinerary.title: Departure from Leh
itinerary.description: Wake up, have breakfast at the hotel, pack bags, and head to Leh airport for the onward journey home.

Perfect for friends, backpackers, and first-time travelers, this budget Ladakh tour lets you experience the region’s most famous landscapes without spending too much. In just six days, you will explore stunning mountain passes, unique valleys, and one of the most beautiful lakes in the Himalayas.Your journey begins in Leh, where you take time to acclimatize before exploring some of the town’s famous attractions. Visit the peaceful Shanti Stupa for panoramic views of the city, witness the optical illusion at Magnetic Hill, and seek blessings at Gurudwara Pathar Sahib. You will also stop at Sangam Point where the Indus and Zanskar rivers meet, and explore the inspiring Hall of Fame museum.The adventure continues as you cross the famous Khardung La to reach the breathtaking Nubra Valley, known for its cold desert, sand dunes, and double-humped camels in Hunder. From here, you travel across high mountain roads to the stunning Pangong Lake, famous for its changing shades of blue. With iconic destinations, thrilling drives, and unforgettable Himalayan views, this cost saving trip is a perfect way to experience the magic of Ladakh. Read MoreItineraryDay 1Arrival in Leh. Leisure Day for Acclimatization.Arrive at the Leh airport after a scenic flight over the Himalayas.Meet our representative who will help you with your transfers to the hotel.Complete the check-in formalities and rest for some time.You can then spend the rest of the day at leisure.We suggest you go for a short walk around your hotel, to acclimate yourself to the altitude better.After coming back from the hotel our trip captain will provide you with a brief of the journey. Overnight stay in Leh.Day 2Leh Local Sightseeing- Visit Gurudwara Pathar Sahib, Shanti Stupa, etc. Wake up in your hotel room in Leh and have a delicious breakfast. After having breakfast, get ready for local sightseeing. Our first stop will be the iconic Shanti Stupa for a mesmerizing view of Leh and its surroundings.Moving forward, we will stop at Gurudwara Pathar Sahib, a sacred place that is believed to have imprints of Sikh Guru Nanak Dev ji.Next, we will also witness the rare phenomenon at the Magnetic Hill where the natural surroundings create an optical illusion that makes it look like vehicles are rolling uphill.Further, we will stop at the Sangam Point where the Zanskar and Indus rivers confluence. Next, we can explore the Hall of Fame, which honors the Indian soldiers who died in the wars and battles fought in Ladakh.You can spend your evening exploring the vibrant local Leh market and indulging in some delicious food and drinks at the cozy cafes around.After a day of exploration and adventure, we will return to our hotel.Enjoy a delicious dinner, and then get some well-deserved rest.Overnight stay in Leh. Day 3Leh To Nubra Valley via Khardung-La - one of the highest motorable roadsWake up in your hotel room in Leh and have a delicious breakfast.We will then get ready for the exciting journey on one of the highest motorable roads.Our first stop will be the iconic Khardung-La - one of the highest motorable roads in the world at a height of 5359 meters.Next, we will stop at the Diskit Monastery, the biggest monastery of Nubra Valley which is famous as the home of 106 feet of Maitreya Buddha statue.Further, we will pass over from Shyok River to reach Nubra Valley, also known as Ldorma or the Valley of Flowers. If time permits, you can also take part in some adventure like a double-humped Bactrian camel safari or an ATV ride on the deserted land of Hunder Sand dunes. title: 6 Days Zanskar Bike Trip from Manali | Monasteries and Mountains
description: A 5-night, 6-day bike expedition from Manali through Shingo La into the remote Zanskar Valley, exploring ancient monasteries like Karsha and Phugtal Gompa, Drang Drung Glacier, and camping under stars near Gonbo Rangjon. One of India's most offbeat Himalayan bike adventures.
highlights: Ride through Atal Tunnel, the world's highest road tunnel above 10,000 ft
Conquer Shingo La Pass at 5,091m
Explore Karsha Monastery, the largest monastery in Zanskar
Views of Drang Drung Glacier from Pensi La
Hike to Phugtal Gompa, a remote cave monastery
Camping under starlit skies near Gonbo Rangjon
Ride through the untouched terrain of Zanskar Valley
Stop at Sani Lake high-altitude lake

itinerary.title: Evening Departure From Delhi To Manali
activities: Overnight bus/travel from Delhi to Manali
itinerary.description: Start from Delhi on Day 0. Reach Manali by morning.

itinerary.title: Arrival in Manali | Ride to Jispa
itinerary.description: Arrive in Manali, freshen up, have breakfast, gear up bikes and ride through the Atal Tunnel into Lahaul Valley, along the Chandra River, arriving at a campsite in Jispa under a starlit sky.

itinerary.title: Jispa to Padum via Shingo La Pass
activities: Arrive at Padum - capital of Zanskar
itinerary.description: Ride from Jispa into the heart of Zanskar Valley via the Shingo La Pass (5,091m), descending into Zanskar's dramatic landscape, passing the hamlet of Purne, and arriving at Padum.

itinerary.title: Padum Exploration | Monasteries, High Pass & Glacier Views
activities: Panoramic views from Pensi La Top
itinerary.description: Explore Zanskar's raw beauty with a visit to Karsha Monastery, a scenic ride to Pensi La with a stop at Sani Lake, and views of the magnificent Drang Drung Glacier before returning to Padum.

itinerary.title: Hike to Phugtal Gompa | Transfer to Gonbo Rangjon
activities: Ride along Zanskar River to Purne
Cross wooden bridges over Tsarap River
itinerary.description: Ride along the Zanskar River route to Purne, hike ~1 hour to the remote cave monastery Phugtal Gompa, explore and interact with monks, then ride to camp near the sacred Gonbo Rangjon peak.

itinerary.title: Gonbo Rangjon to Manali | Leisure Day
activities: Ride back via Shingo La Pass
Leisure evening in Old Manali / cafe hopping
itinerary.description: Early morning ride back to Manali via Shingo La Pass, passing through Darcha and Keylong. Submit bikes, check in to hotel, and spend the evening at leisure in Old Manali.

itinerary.title: Self-Exploration of Manali | Evening Depart to Delhi
activities: Visit Vashisht Temple and hot springs
Shopping and eating at Mall Road
Depart from Manali bus stop to Delhi
itinerary.description: Leisurely morning in Manali with optional visits to Hidimba Temple, Vashisht Temple hot springs, and Mall Road shopping. Depart by bus to Delhi in the evening.

itinerary.title: Reach Delhi by Morning
itinerary.description: Arrive back in Delhi by morning, concluding the Zanskar bike expedition.

raw_text: <iframe src="https://www.googletagmanager.com/ns.html?id=GTM-WM6XH8RL" height="0" width="0" style="display:none;visibility:hidden"></iframe> Luxury ExperiencesUpcoming TripsCorporate ToursBlogsAbout UsPayments+91-8130288566+91-8130288566International TripsEuropeVietnamBaliThailandJapanKenyaIcelandGeorgiaSri LankaBhutanPhilippinesEgyptTurkeyKazakhstanMaldivesSouth AfricaSingaporeNew ZealandFranceSwitzerlandSpainDubaiAustraliaMalaysiaMauritiusIndia TripsLadakhSpitiLadakh Bike TripSpiti Bike TripMeghalayaKashmirSikkimHimachal PradeshUttarakhandArunachal PradeshAndamanRajasthanKeralaNagalandGroup ToursEuropeLadakhVietnamJapanSri LankaPhilippinesDubaiThailandGeorgiaBhutanArunachal PradeshAndamanSpiti ValleyHimachal PradeshBaliMeghalayaKazakhstanKashmirEvents & FestivalsNEWWeekend GetawaysLuxury ExperiencesEvents & FestivalsNEWUpcoming Community TripsInternational TripsIndia TripsGroup TripsWeekend GetawaysBike TripsOther TripsBlogsCareersAbout UsPaymentsContact UsDownload ItineraryShare this link viaOr copy linkcopy6 Days Zanskar Bike Trip from Manali | Monasteries and MountainsPickup & DropDelhi - DelhiDuration5N - 6DOverview & HighlightsItineraryInclusionsExclusionsOther InfoOverview & HighlightsManali - Jispa - Padum - Pensi La - Gonbo Rongjon - Manali - DelhiIf you think you’ve seen the best of Ladakh, then you haven’t seen Zanskar Valley. Tucked deep within the Himalayas and cut off for most of the year, this is one of India’s last truly offbeat frontiers and the best way to experience it is on two wheels. The journey begins from Manali as we ride through the iconic Atal Tunnel into the raw landscapes of Lahaul, eventually reaching Jispa under a sky full of stars. The real adventure kicks in as we conquer the mighty Shingo La at 5,091m, riding into the untouched terrain of Zanskar where roads turn rugged and views turn unreal. In Zanskar, we explore ancient monasteries like Karsha Monastery, ride to the breathtaking Drang Drung Glacier via Pensi La, and take on a once-in-a-lifetime hike to the surreal Phugtal Monastery - a hidden cave monastery carved into the mountains. We then ride to the sacred Gonbo Rangjon, camping under star-lit skies beside this mystical peak before making our way back to Manali. This isn’t just a bike trip - it’s a ride into the wild, untouched soul of the Himalayas. Book now with WanderOn and answer the call of Zanskar. Read MoreItineraryDay 0 Evening Departure From Delhi To ManaliStart from Delhi on Day 0. Reach Manali by morning.Day 1Arrival in Manali | Ride to JispaArrive in Manali, the "Valley of Gods" and gear up for the adventure aheadFreshen up, fuel yourself with a hearty breakfast, and get your bikes ready to rollWe ride out through the legendary Atal Tunnel, the world's highest road tunnel above 10,000 ft.We will then watch the landscape transform as we enter the Lahaul Valley.Ride along the mighty Chandra River and enjoy the views of the towering peaks at the backdrop. We arrive at our campsite in Jispa and settle in for the evening.Enjoy a cozy evening and fall asleep under the sky filled with countless stars covering the Lahaul Valley, feeling refreshed and rejuvenated for the adventures that lie ahead.Overnight stay in JispaDay 2Jispa to Padum via Shingo La PassWake up to a beautiful morning in the mountains and have your breakfast.Next, we will get ready for an offbeat ride into the heart of the Zanskar Valley — a route very few riders ever explore.Today, we will pass the famous Shingo La Pass (also known as Shinku La) located at an altitude of 5,091m.Enjoy this thrilling ride through this pass that connects Himachal Pradesh to Ladakh and watch some jaw-dropping views.We will then descend into Zanskar's dramatic landscape and pass the small hamlet of Purne.We will also take quick scenic stops to soak in the beauty of the place.Reach Padum - the capital of the Zanskar region, and check-in to the hotel.Enjoy your dinner at the stay and call it a day.Overnight stay in Padum.Day 3Padum Exploration | Monasteries, High Pass & Glacier ViewsWake up to a peaceful morning in Padum and have your breakfast.Today, we explore the raw beauty of Zanskar as we begin our sightseeing around Padum.Start the day with a visit to the iconic Karsha Monastery, the largest monastery in the region, known for its stunning hilltop location and spiritual significance.Post the visit, we will take a scenic ride towards the mighty Pensi La, gradually ascending through dramatic mountain landscapes. En route, we will take a short stop at Sani Lake, a high-altitude lake perfect for a quick break and some pictures.As we reach Pensi La Top, soak in the breathtaking panoramic views of the surrounding peaks and valleys. From here, we continue ahead to witness the magnificent Drang Drung Glacier, one of the largest glaciers in Ladakh, offering a truly surreal sight.After spending some time admiring the views, we will begin our journey back towards Padum.Overnight stay in Padum.Day 4Hike to the Phugtal Gompa

title: 7 Days Thrilling Ladakh Tour with Umling La - Classic Value Circuit
destination: Ladakh (Leh - Sham Valley - Khardung-La - Nubra - Pangong - Hanle - Umling-La - Leh)
description: A 6-night, 7-day road circuit starting and ending in Leh, covering iconic Ladakh highlights including Khardung La, Nubra Valley, Pangong Tso, Hanle, and the world's highest motorable pass, Umling La at 5640 meters. The trip offers thrilling high-altitude roads, cold deserts, blue lakes, and bonfire nights in simple, comfortable stays.
highlights: Umling La - world's highest motorable pass at 5640 meters
Khardung La - one of the highest motorable roads at 5359 meters
Nubra Valley cold desert and Hunder Sand Dunes
Pangong Tso - highest saltwater lake in the world
Hanle village with clear night skies
Rezang La War Memorial
Diskit Monastery with 106-ft Maitreya Buddha
Bonfire experiences
Shanti Stupa, Magnetic Hill, and Pathar Sahib Gurudwara

itinerary.title: Arrival in Leh - Leisure Day for Acclimatization
activities: Leisure walk around hotel for acclimatization
itinerary.description: Arrive at Leh airport after a scenic flight over the Himalayas. Meet the representative, transfer to hotel, check in, rest, and take a short walk around the hotel to acclimatize.

itinerary.title: Leh Local Sightseeing - Sham Valley Exploration
activities: Sangam Point - Indus and Zanskar confluence
itinerary.description: After breakfast, explore Leh's major landmarks including Shanti Stupa, Sangam Point (confluence of Indus and Zanskar Rivers), Magnetic Hill, Pathar Sahib Gurudwara, and Hall of Fame war memorial. Evening at leisure in Leh market.

itinerary.title: Leh to Nubra Valley via Khardung-La
activities: Diskit Monastery with 106-ft Maitreya Buddha statue
itinerary.description: Drive approximately 125 km (5-6 hours) from Leh to Nubra Valley crossing Khardung La at 5359 meters, one of the highest motorable roads in the world. Visit Diskit Monastery and Hunder Sand Dunes with optional camel or ATV ride.

itinerary.title: Nubra to Pangong via Shyok Valley
activities: Scenic drive through Agam and Shyok villages
Check-in at campsite near Pangong Lake
itinerary.description: Drive approximately 160 km (5-6 hours) through Agam and Shyok villages to reach Pangong Tso, the highest saltwater lake in the world at 4300 meters altitude.

itinerary.title: Pangong to Hanle via Rezang La War Memorial
activities: Loma Bridge crossing on Indus River
Dinner and overnight stay at homestay in Hanle
itinerary.description: Drive approximately 165 km (8-9 hours) with an early sunrise at Pangong, passing Chushul, Rezang La War Memorial (1962 Indo-China War tribute), Loma Bridge on Indus River, arriving at Hanle village by evening.

itinerary.title: Excursion to Umling La and Depart for Leh
activities: Ride to Umling La (5640 m) - world's highest motorable road
Sightseeing and photography at Umling La
Return drive to Leh via Koyul, Loma, Nyoma, Mahe, Chumathang, Upshi, Karu
itinerary.description: Early morning ride to Umling La, the world's highest motorable pass at 5640 meters, crossing Photi La (5524 m) en route. Return to Leh via Koyul, Loma, Nyoma, Mahe, Chumathang, Upshi, and Karu — approximately 295 km in 8-9 hours.

itinerary.title: Departure from Leh
itinerary.description: Final day in Leh with departure.

Starting and ending in Leh, you will ride through Sham Valley, cross Khardung La - one of the highest motorable passes in the world and reach the beautiful Nubra Valley with its cold desert and sand dunes. The trip takes you next to the stunning Pangong Tso Lake, where the water changes colour as the sun moves across the sky. After Pangong, you continue towards Hanle, a quiet village surrounded by mountains and clear night skies. Then comes the most exciting part - a full-day ride to Umling La, the highest motorable pass on Earth with huge views that feel like the world is right under your feet. Throughout these 7 days, you will travel on thrilling roads, stay in simple and comfortable places, share stories around a bonfire, and see landscapes that change from green valleys to rocky mountains and blue lakes. This tour gives you the real feel of Ladakh’s nature and open roads, without extra frills - just great routes, great views and great memories. Read MoreItineraryDay 1Arrival in Leh. It's time to fuel up for an exciting day ahead with a delicious breakfast at the hotel.Get ready and board our transfers to explore the major landmarks towards Sham Valley.Before heading out of Leh, we will first visit the iconic Shanti Stupa to marvel at the stunning views of Leh city.Continue the adventurous ride to the Sham Valley, where we will first visit the Sangam point to witness the confluence of the Indus and Zanskar Rivers. the serene Pathar Sahib Gurudwara.Next, we will stop at the Magnetic Hill to experience a unique optical illusion that looks like vehicles are rolling uphill.Our last stop here will be the Pathar Sahib Gurudwara, dedicated to Guru Nanak Dev Ji.Here, we will learn about the fascinating story behind the sacred boulder that has an imprint of Guru Nanak Ji. We will then head back to Leh and visit the Hall of Fame, a museum/ war memorial built in memory of the martyrs of the Indo-Pak War that showcases various aspects of high-altitude warfare. You can spend your evening exploring the vibrant local Leh market and indulging in some delicious food and drinks at the cozy cafes around.Overnight stay in Leh.Day 3Leh To Nubra Valley via Khardung-La [Distance: 125km, Duration: 5-6 Hours]. Wake up in your hotel room in Leh and have a delicious breakfast.We will then get you ready for the exciting journey on one of the highest motorable roads.Rev Up your engines and get ready for a thrilling ride to the Nubra Valley located to the north of Ladakh between the Karakoram and Ladakh ranges of the Himalayas.Our first stop will be the iconic Khardung-La - one of the highest motorable roads in the world at an altitude of 5359 meters.Further, we will pass by the Shyok River to reach Nubra Valley, also known as Ldorma or the Valley of Flowers.Continue riding through the mesmerizing landscapes until we reach Diskit.Here, we will stop at the Diskit Monastery which is famous for its 106 feet of Maitreya Buddha statue. title: 8-Day Leh Ladakh Tour Package with Turtuk and Tso Moriri
description: An 8-day Leh to Leh tour covering Khardung La, Nubra Valley, Turtuk Village, Pangong Lake, and Tso Moriri. The journey traverses some of the world's highest motorable roads with stunning high-altitude lakes, sand dunes, and remote villages.
highlights: Cross Khardung La, one of the world's highest motorable passes at 5359m
Explore remote Turtuk Village near the Pakistan border
Camp by Pangong Lake at 4300m altitude
Traverse high-altitude Kaksang La pass at 17851 ft
Visit Tso Moriri lake with rare migratory birds and flora
Bactrian camel safari at Hunder Sand Dunes
Witness geothermal activity at Puga Valley hot springs
Explore Diskit Monastery and Maitreya Buddha statue

itinerary.title: Leh Arrival and Acclimatisation Day
itinerary.description: Reach Leh and check into your hotel. Rest, walk around, and drink plenty of water to avoid altitude sickness.

itinerary.title: Leh Local Sightseeing
activities: Explore Hall of Fame war memorial
Seek blessings at Pathar Sahib Gurudwara
itinerary.description: Explore Leh's local attractions including Shanti Stupa, Hall of Fame, Pathar Sahib Gurudwara, Magnetic Hill, and Sangam Point, followed by street food at Leh Market.

itinerary.title: Leh to Nubra Valley via Khardung La
activities: Optional Bactrian camel safari at Nubra Valley
Visit Diskit Monastery and Maitreya Buddha statue
itinerary.description: Ride 125 km (5-6 hours) over Khardung La at 5359m to reach Nubra Valley, visiting Diskit Monastery and Hunder Sand Dunes.

itinerary.title: Day Excursion to Turtuk from Nubra Valley
itinerary.description: A 200 km (7-8 hour) day excursion to remote Turtuk Village, known for its ethnic Muslim community, apricots, walnuts, and the Shyok War Memorial.

itinerary.title: Nubra Valley to Pangong Tso via Shyok Valley
activities: Photography at lake including 3 Idiots film set
itinerary.description: Ride 160 km (5-6 hours) through Agam and Shyok villages to reach Pangong Lake at 4300m altitude, famous for its crystal-clear blue waters.

itinerary.title: Pangong Tso to Tso Moriri via Kaksang La, Kyagar Tso & Yaya Tso
activities: Pass Yaya Tso and Kyagar Tso
itinerary.description: 175 km (8-9 hours) journey crossing Chusul, Mirpal Tso, Kaksang La pass (17851 ft), Yaya Tso, and Kyagar Tso before arriving at the magnificent Tso Moriri lake.

itinerary.title: Tso Moriri to Leh via Puga Valley and Chumathang
activities: Visit Puga Valley geothermal springs and mud pools
itinerary.description: 154 km (7-8 hours) return journey to Leh passing through Puga Valley's geothermal hot springs, sulphur deposits, and Chumathang.

itinerary.title: Leh Departure
itinerary.description: Final day in Leh with departure transfers.

title: Best Ladakh Bike Trip from Srinagar to Manali | Umling La | Tso Moriri
destination: Srinagar to Manali via Leh, Hanle, Umling La
description: An epic 11-day Ladakh biking expedition from Srinagar to Manali covering high-altitude passes like Khardung La and Umling La (world's highest motorable road), stunning lakes like Pangong and Tso Moriri, and remote villages across the Himalayas. Riders traverse legendary routes including Nubra Valley, Hanle, Demchok, and Sarchu before concluding in Manali.
highlights: Conquer Umling La — the highest motorable road in the world
Ride through Khardung La (5359m)
Cruise alongside Pangong Lake and Tso Moriri
Explore Nubra Valley and Hunder Sand Dunes
Visit Rezang La War Memorial (1962 Indo-China War tribute)
Cross Zoji La, Namika La, and Fotu La passes
Traverse the legendary Moore Plains and 21-bend Gata Loops
Ride through the Atal Tunnel to Manali
Experience ancient monasteries like Diskit and Lamayuru
Starry skies and campfire bonding at Sarchu
Spot migratory birds at Tso Moriri

itinerary.title: Arrival in Srinagar – Day at Leisure
itinerary.description: Arrive in Srinagar, the summer capital of Jammu and Kashmir. Check in to your houseboat, explore Srinagar Market on your own, and attend a briefing session with the Team Captain.

itinerary.title: Srinagar to Kargil via Sonamarg, Drass & Zojila
itinerary.description: Ride approximately 200km over 8-9 hours from Srinagar to Kargil, crossing the meadows of Sonamarg (ancient Silk Road), conquering Zoji La pass (3528m), and passing through Drass — the coldest inhabited place in India.

itinerary.title: Kargil to Leh – En Route Major Landmarks
itinerary.description: Ride approximately 230km over 7-8 hours from Kargil to Leh, crossing Namika La (3700m) and Fotu La (4108m), passing Lamayuru's moon-like landscape, Sangam Point (confluence of Indus and Zanskar rivers), Magnetic Hill, and Pathar Sahib Gurudwara.

itinerary.title: Leh to Nubra Valley via Khardung La
itinerary.description: Ride approximately 125km over 5-6 hours from Leh to Nubra Valley, stopping at Shanti Stupa for views of Leh, conquering Khardung La (5359m), visiting Diskit Monastery with its 106-feet Maitreya Buddha statue, and exploring Hunder Sand Dunes.

itinerary.title: Nubra to Pangong via Shyok Valley
activities: Ride through Agam and Shyok villages
Check-in at campsite near Pangong Lake
itinerary.description: Ride approximately 160km over 5-6 hours through Agam and Shyok villages to reach the majestic Pangong Lake — the world's highest saltwater lake at 4300m altitude, approximately 136km long and 4km wide.

itinerary.title: Pangong to Hanle via Rezang La War Memorial
activities: Cross Loma Bridge on Indus River
itinerary.description: Ride approximately 165km over 8-9 hours from Pangong to Hanle village, passing through Chushul, stopping at Rezang La War Memorial (tribute to soldiers of 1962 Indo-China War), and crossing Loma Bridge on the Indus River.

itinerary.title: Day Excursion to Umling La | Visit Demchok
activities: Ride to Umling La (world's highest motorable road)
Return to Hanle for overnight stay
itinerary.description: Approximately 200km day excursion over 7-8 hours to Umling La — the world's highest motorable road — and visit to Demchok village near the India-China border.

raw_text: <iframe src="https://www.googletagmanager.com/ns.html?id=GTM-WM6XH8RL" height="0" width="0" style="display:none;visibility:hidden"></iframe> Luxury ExperiencesUpcoming TripsCorporate ToursBlogsAbout UsPayments+91-8130288566+91-8130288566International TripsEuropeVietnamBaliThailandJapanKenyaIcelandGeorgiaSri LankaBhutanPhilippinesEgyptTurkeyKazakhstanMaldivesSouth AfricaSingaporeNew ZealandFranceSwitzerlandSpainDubaiAustraliaMalaysiaMauritiusIndia TripsLadakhSpitiLadakh Bike TripSpiti Bike TripMeghalayaKashmirSikkimHimachal PradeshUttarakhandArunachal PradeshAndamanRajasthanKeralaNagalandGroup ToursEuropeLadakhVietnamJapanSri LankaPhilippinesDubaiThailandGeorgiaBhutanArunachal PradeshAndamanSpiti ValleyHimachal PradeshBaliMeghalayaKazakhstanKashmirEvents & FestivalsNEWWeekend GetawaysLuxury ExperiencesEvents & FestivalsNEWUpcoming Community TripsInternational TripsIndia TripsGroup TripsWeekend GetawaysBike TripsOther TripsBlogsCareersAbout UsPaymentsContact UsDownload ItineraryShare this link viaOr copy linkcopyBest Ladakh Bike Trip from Srinagar to Manali | Umling La | Tso MoririPickup & DropSrinagar - DelhiDuration10N - 11DOverview & HighlightsItineraryInclusionsExclusionsOther InfoOverview & HighlightsSrinagar - Kargil - Leh - Khardung-La - Nubra - Pangong - Hanle - Umling-La - Demchok - Tso Moriri - Leh - Sarchu - Manali - DelhiTick Off the Bucket List Destination of Every Biker as you get ready for the ultimate adventure of a lifetime — a thrilling 12-day Ladakh Biking Expedition that will leave you with unforgettable memories. Whether you're riding solo or with your best buddies, this epic journey promises breathtaking views, heart-racing rides, and moments you'll treasure forever. Feel the rush as you conquer high-altitude passes like Zoji La, Khardung La, and the mighty Umling La — the highest motorable road in the world! Cruise alongside shimmering blue lakes like Pangong and Tso Moriri, where nature reveals its most stunning colors. Ride through valleys filled with apricot orchards in Turtuk and experience the peace of ancient monasteries like Diskit. Explore rugged terrains, winding roads, and surreal landscapes as you cross the legendary Moore Plains, the treacherous Gata Loops with 21 hairpin bends, and the scenic Atal Tunnel leading to Manali.Witness the bravery of soldiers at Rezang La War Memorial, spot migratory birds at Tso Moriri, and relax under starry skies at Sarchu. Bond with fellow bikers around campfires, share laughter, and create stories worth telling. This isn't just a bike trip; it's your chance to conquer Ladakh’s wild beauty, challenge yourself, and soak in the magic of the Himalayas. So rev up your engines — an epic adventure awaits!Read MoreItineraryDay 1Arrival in Srinagar. Day at Leisure in in Srinagar.Reach Srinagar - the summer capital of Jammu and Kashmir and head to your stay.Reach your houseboat and complete the check-in formalities.You can then explore the Srinagar Market on your own and return to your stay.You will then attend a short briefing session with the Team Captain for the next day.Overnight stay at Srinagar. Day 2Srinagar To Kargil Via Sonamarg, Drass & Zojila. [Distance: 200km, Duration: 8-9 Hours].Wake up to a pleasant morning in Srinagar and have your breakfast at the hotel.Get ready for an exciting day as we depart for the scenic town of Kargil.As we start our journey, we will cross the beautiful meadows of Sonmarg, which was part of the ancient Silk Road. Next, it's time to conquer the Zoji La, one of the most challenging and breathtaking mountain passes in India at an altitude of 3528m.As the journey comes to an end, we will cross Drass village, often referred to as the Gateway to Ladakh and also known as the coldest inhabited place in India.Continue the journey to Kargil and check in to the hotel upon reaching.Have a delicious dinner and rest for the day. Overnight stay in Kargil.Day 3Kargil To Leh. En route explore some major landmarks. title: Kareri Lake Trek with McLeodganj: 3 Days Trip From Delhi
description: A 3-day weekend trek from Delhi to Kareri Lake via McLeodganj, featuring a guided trek alongside the Nyund River to a glacial lake at 2,934m with dome camp stays under the Dhauladhar peaks. Includes sightseeing at Bhagsunag Temple and Bhagsu Waterfall in McLeodganj.

Trek alongside Nyund River through scenic forest trail

Not too long, not too tough, but the kind of trip that genuinely surprises you at every turn. You’ll leave Delhi at night, reach Dharamshala by morning, and honestly by the time you've had breakfast and your boots are on, the city already feels like a different life. The trail runs right alongside the Nyund River the whole way up to the base camp, which sounds like a small detail but it's actually what makes the first day so good. Camp at Kareri Base Camp is a whole different experience. Dome tents, a proper bonfire, the Dhauladhar peaks turning pink at sunset, it's the kind of evening where nobody's really on their phone and conversations just happen naturally. The climb to Kareri Lake takes you above the treeline completely, and suddenly it's just you, the snow-capped Dhauladhar range in every direction, and this incredibly still glacial lake sitting at nearly 3,000 metres. It's cold, it's quiet, and it's genuinely one of the prettiest things you'll see on a weekend trek from Delhi. McLeodGanj gives you a slow pace with iconic highlights like Bhagsunag Temple and Bhagsu Waterfall. Don’t miss the momos, Tibetan chai, and a few hours of wandering before the road back. This 3 days Kareri Lake trek package has a funny way of feeling like you were gone for two weeks. title: Manali to Srinagar Ladakh Trip with Umling La & Nubra Valley - Pocket Saver
description: An 11-day road journey from Manali to Srinagar covering the highest motorable pass in the world (Umling La), Nubra Valley, Pangong Tso, Hanle, and Kargil. The trip traverses iconic Himalayan highways, high-altitude passes, remote valleys, and stunning lakes.

Manali to Srinagar full road journey

Starting from Manali in Himachal Pradesh, you ride through winding mountain roads and the Atal Tunnel into remote high-altitude lands. Soon you reach Sarchu and then Leh, the heart of Ladakh, where the landscape turns wide, open, and dramatic. You then move to the Nubra Valley over the famous Khardung La pass, where sand dunes and river valleys make everything feel different from anywhere else. From Nubra, the road takes you to the blue waters of Pangong Tso Lake, one of the most iconic lakes in the region. After Pangong, the trip heads further east with a scenic journey to Hanle Village - a quiet and peaceful highland surrounded by big mountain views.A major highlight is the full-day ride up to Umling La, known as the highest motorable pass in the world. The views from up there feel huge, and the experience of reaching that altitude is unforgettable. From Hanle you return to Leh and continue the road south into Kargil and then into Srinagar, where your journey ends with memories of high mountains, wide valleys, lakes, and long open roads. It has a little something for everyone! Anyone looking for that extra dose of an adrenaline rush or a rejuvenating experience can find that in Himachal. To give our fellow wanderers a bit of thrill and chill, we've curated an itinerary that would satiate the wanderlust in you! This trip will start from Mcleodganj, the residence of His Holiness the 14th Dalai Lama. Visit beautiful Tibetan monasteries, satisfy your tastebuds with Indo-Italo-Israeli-Tibetan cuisine, and witness the amalgamation of Indo-Tibetan culture. The next day, you will reach Bir, the Paragliding capital of our India, to feel the thrill as you take off to paraglide. Then, the trip would take you to the beautiful valley of Tirthan, a less explored gem of Himachal, a perfect place to plan a leisure trip. From there, you will start your journey to Jalori pass to experience the 360-degree view of the Himalayan mountains and trek further to mesmerizing Serolsar lake. This trip will give you a thrilling as well as a rejuvenating experience.Read MoreItineraryDay 0Evening Departure From Delhi To McleodganjStart from Delhi on Day 0. This Shangarh weekend trip takes you to one of the most beautiful hidden villages in Himachal Pradesh. Tucked inside the Great Himalayan National Park region, Shangarh is famous for its wide green meadows, wooden temples, and peaceful mountain views. The trip begins with an overnight journey from Delhi or Chandigarh to Aut, followed by a scenic drive to the charming village of Shangarh. During the trip, you will explore the magical Barshangarh Waterfall, visit the sacred Shangchul Mahadev Temple, and spend relaxing time in the beautiful Shangarh meadow surrounded by forests. The adventure continues with a short trek to the stunning Pundrik Rishi Lake, a peaceful alpine lake hidden among forests and mountains. The calm surroundings make it a perfect place to relax and enjoy nature. On the final day, you will visit the scenic Raila village and Rupi Raila Waterfall, followed by an optional river rafting experience for some added thrill. If you love offbeat places, forest treks, and peaceful mountain views, this Shangarh trip with WanderOn is the perfect weekend escape. Read MoreItineraryDay 0Departure from Delhi/Chandigarh. title: Spiti Valley Circuit Bike Trip
description: An 8-day biking expedition from Tirthan Valley to Manali covering high-altitude passes, ancient monasteries, and remote villages of the Spiti Valley. Riders traverse challenging Himalayan terrain including Chitkul, Kalpa, Kaza, Key Monastery, Hikkim, Langza, and Chandratal Lake.
highlights: Ride through high-altitude Spiti Valley on Royal Enfield Himalayan 450 CC
Visit Key Monastery perched atop a hill
Send postcards from Hikkim, the world's highest post office
Explore fossil-rich Langza Village
Camp overnight at Chandratal Lake under the Milky Way
Visit Komic, the highest village in Asia at 4513 m
Cross Chicham Bridge, the highest suspension bridge in Asia
Ride through Kinnaur Valley along the Satluj River
Visit Chitkul, the last Indian village near the China border

itinerary.title: Departure from Delhi to Tirthan Valley
activities: Overnight Volvo bus journey from Delhi
itinerary.description: Overnight journey from Delhi to Aut by Volvo bus. Approximately 470 km, 11-12 hours.

itinerary.title: Reach Aut - Transfer to Gushaini/Jibhi
itinerary.description: Arrive at Aut, transfer to Gushaini/Jibhi. Bikers head to Manali to collect bikes and do a test ride to Gushaini/Jibhi. Backpackers trek to Choie/Jibhi waterfall.

itinerary.title: Departure to Chitkul via Kinnaur Valley
itinerary.description: Early morning departure to Chitkul, the last Indian village towards the China border, through Kinnaur Valley along the Satluj River. Approximately 230 km, 10-11 hours.

itinerary.title: Explore Chitkul - Transfer to Kalpa
itinerary.description: Early morning sunrise experience in Chitkul, explore the local village, then depart for Kalpa by noon. Approximately 80 km, 6-7 hours.

itinerary.title: Kalpa to Kaza via Nako - Tabo - Ka Loops
activities: Enter Spiti Valley via Sumdo Border
itinerary.description: Early morning departure through Sumdo Border into Spiti Valley, visiting Nako and Tabo villages and crossing the Ka loops en route to Kaza. Approximately 220 km, 8-9 hours.

itinerary.title: Visit Key Monastery - Hikkim - Komic - Langza
activities: Visit Chicham Bridge (highest suspension bridge in Asia)
Visit Komic village (4513 m, highest village in Asia)
Send postcards from Hikkim (world's highest post office)
itinerary.description: Explore Key Monastery, Chicham Bridge, Komic village, Hikkim post office, and Langza fossil site. Approximately 80 km, 6-7 hours. Overnight stay in Kaza.

itinerary.title: Depart for Chandratal Lake
itinerary.description: After breakfast, depart for Chandratal Lake with a pit stop at Losar, the last village of Spiti Valley. Camp overnight under the stars. Approximately 95 km, 5-6 hours.

itinerary.title: Depart for Manali - Old Manali Café Crawl
itinerary.description: Early morning departure from Chandratal to Manali. Explore Old Manali cafés in the evening. Approximately 111 km, 6-7 hours.

itinerary.title: Self-Explore Manali - Departure to Delhi
itinerary.description: Morning self-exploration of Manali before departing for Delhi by evening. Approximately 510 km, 10-11 hours overnight journey.

itinerary.title: Reach Delhi
itinerary.description: Arrive back in Delhi by morning with trip memories.

If you too are one of those who have a passion to ride on seemingly endless roads, then perhaps a bike trip to Spiti Valley is all that you need. Spiti Valley is a high altitude desert whose beauty unveils after a tough and tricky ride through the most challenging roads of Himalayas. If you feel the need for adrenaline, you got your share of dose right throughout your trip to Spiti. A bike expedition to Spiti can give you the feel of riding through the passes of Leh Ladakh, as this place is popularly called the mini version of it! As you begin your journey to Spiti from Manali, you travel through the popular Rohtang Pass located in the Pir Panjal Range of Himalayas. This ride will feed your appetite for thrill and vast soul-satiating panoramas. As you leave behind the chaos of cities, Spiti Valley will happily embrace you into a zone of peace and love. Soak in the holy vibes at Key Monastery, which is an important place of pilgrimage for the Buddhist population residing in Spiti. Explore the long lost stories in the fossils of Langza Village and witness the grandeur of the golden statue of Buddha. Ride upto the highest post office in the world at Hikkim and send a postcard to your loved ones and also to yourself! In short, just forget about the metropolis life for a few days and spend some time in a natural retreat at this high altitude desert! Our Bikes are already vrooming and we are all set to take you to this less explored paradise through our Spiti Valley Tour Packages for 2025! Time to get #Spitified!Read MoreItineraryDay 0Departure from Delhi to Tirthan Valley. title: Spiti Valley Summer Trip 5 Days | Best Time to Explore Little Tibet
description: A 5-day road trip through Spiti Valley covering Key Monastery, Hikkim's world's highest post office, Komic village, Langza fossils, and the stunning Chandratal Lake. The journey travels from Delhi to Manali and deep into the high-altitude cold desert landscape of Spiti during peak summer months (June–September).
highlights: Thrilling drive through Rohtang Pass and Atal Tunnel
Cross Kunzum La at 14,931 ft
Visit Key Monastery — world-famous Buddhist center of learning
Send postcards from the world's highest post office at Hikkim
Visit Komic — highest village in Asia at 4,513 m
Explore marine fossils at Langza
Chicham Bridge — highest suspension bridge in Asia
Camp overnight at Chandratal (Moon Lake) at 14,000 ft
Stargazing and Milky Way views at Chandratal
Experience transition from green valleys to brown arid desert
price_includes: 4 nights accommodation (1 night hotel at Manali, 2 nights hotel/homestay at Kaza, 1 night Swiss tents at Chandratal)
7 meals throughout the trip (Day 1 breakfast, Day 2 dinner, Day 3 breakfast & dinner, Day 4 breakfast & dinner, Day 5 breakfast)

itinerary.title: Departure from Delhi to Manali
activities: Overnight journey from Delhi to Manali
itinerary.description: Leave from Delhi by evening and reach Manali the next morning.

itinerary.title: Reach Manali – Self Exploration – Overnight Stay at Manali
activities: Visit Vashisht Temple and hot water spring
itinerary.description: Arrive in Manali by morning, check in to the hotel, have breakfast, and explore the local area at leisure. Return to hotel by evening for briefing.

itinerary.title: Manali to Kaza via Atal Tunnel and Kunzum La
activities: Visit Chicham Bridge (highest suspension bridge in Asia)
Arrive and overnight stay at Kaza
itinerary.description: Early morning drive from Manali to Kaza (185 km, 6–7 hours) crossing the Atal Tunnel, Kunzum La pass at 14,931 ft, and the Chicham Bridge — the highest suspension bridge in Asia.

itinerary.title: Key Monastery, Hikkim, Komic and Langza – Overnight Stay at Kaza
activities: Send postcards from world's highest post office at Hikkim
Visit Komic village (highest village in Asia, 4,513 m)
itinerary.description: Day excursion (~80 km, 6–7 hours) visiting Key Monastery, the world's highest post office at Hikkim, Komic village (highest village in Asia at 4,513 m), and fossil-rich Langza.

itinerary.title: Kaza to Chandratal Lake via Losar and Kunzum La
activities: Visit and experience Chandratal Lake (14,000 ft)
Check in to campsite Swiss tents
itinerary.description: Drive from Kaza (~95 km, 5–6 hours) to the mystical Chandratal (Moon Lake) at 14,000 ft via Losar and Kunzum La. Overnight stay in Swiss tents under the Milky Way.

itinerary.title: Chandratal to Manali – Departure to Delhi
itinerary.description: Drive back to Manali (510 km, 10–11 hours), rest, then board Volvo bus to Delhi overnight.

A Spiti Valley Trip is a traveler’s delight, thanks to the thrilling roadways and rustic landscapes. While most parts of India undergo unbearably high temperatures, the beauty of Spiti shines like a diamond under sun during peak summer months. The best time to visit Spiti is from June-September when the glacial lakes will welcome you in all their glory. It is now that you hit those roads with our Spiti Valley RoadTrip Package! The treacherous curves that lead to the valley of Spiti from Manali will give you a dose of adventure and beauty all at once. This is your chance to experience the thrill of traveling through the famous Rohtang Pass. Don’t forget to notice your surroundings because a beautiful change is taking place in your surroundings. It is here you’ll notice landscapes change from shades of green to brown. When you finally enter Spiti Valley you’ll realize that it is much more than just a picturesque place of Himalayas. There’s an abundance of rich Buddhist-Tibetan culture that resides in the serrated villages of Spiti. The Key Monastery is one fine example of architecture that makes you adore the concept of simplicity in Buddhism. Other than the skies, there stands another blue marvel on your Trip to Spiti-The Chandratal Lake! Take your time to absorb peace at this splendid glacial lake and click some postcard-perfect shots here. Speaking of postcards, why not send a letter to your loved ones from the World’s highest post office! Pretty special right! So now that you know what to expect on this voyage to the high altitude desert, pack your bags and book yourself a slot in our Spiti Roadtrip Package for 2025!Read MoreItineraryDay 0Departure from Delhi to ManaliLeave from Delhi by evening & reach Manali next morning.Day 1Reach Manali - Manali Self exploration - Overnight stay at Manali.Reach Manali by morning and check-in to the Hotel.After having breakfast and some leisure, feel free to explore the local area of Manali according to the place that interests you.You can visit the Mall road, Hidimba Temple, Old Manali for some good cafes to chill or Vashisht temple for the Hot water Spring.Come back to the hotel by evening.Briefing about the next day and sleep overnight.Day 2Start your journey from Manali to Kaza via Atal Tunnel and Kunzum La. Overnight stay at Kaza. [ Distance: 185 km, Duration: 6 - 7 Hours ]Early morning, we start our journey to the valley of the Gods – Lahaul & Spiti!Crossover to Lahaul by crossing the Engineering Marvel - Atal Tunnel, and watch the terrain shift from lush green valleys to brown arid deserts.At 14,931 ft we cross another high altitude pass & the gateway to the Spiti Valley – Kunzum La.On the way, visit the highest suspension bridge in Asia - The Chicham Bridge.We continue our drive through the stunning landscape to reach Kaza by evening, and overnight stay in hotel.Day 3Visit Key Monastery, Hikkim, Komic and Langza. Overnight stay at Kaza.
